Football.title: Cole Palmer’s girlfriend joins no-bra club as she flaunts curves on Egypt holiday while Chelsea ace prepared for Arsenal
Football.content:
COLE PALMER’S girlfriend Olivia Holder has joined the no-bra club as she flaunted her curves on holiday.

The Chelsea star’s Wag took some time away from England as she jetted off for a break in Egypt.





Olivia, 23, was snapped with Palmer at Winter Wonderland in December 2025.

She has a sizeable social media following, with nearly 20,000 on Instagram thanks to her work as a jet-setting influencer.

The stunning blonde regularly wows fans with glamorous photos on the platform

Her recent trip to Egypt saw her post snaps from a night out in a daring long black dress.

Olivia shared two pictures of herself without a bra, completing the look with a gold bangle and a black Chanel handbag.

Followers loved the snaps as they flooded the comments section.

One posted: “The most beautiful.”

A second wrote: “If Cole loves you, we all love you.”



A third commented: “Obsessed with you always.”

While another said: “My forever beauty queen.”

The influencer regularly gives followers a look into her glam lifestyle including sunshine holidays and fashion.

She has shared recent pictures from the likes of Dubai, the Maldives, Crete and Bucharest.



Palmer, 23, has been at Cobham preparing for Chelsea’s Premier League clash against Arsenal.

The Blues travel to North London to take on the title favourites as they look to boost their Champions League hopes.



The attacking midfielder is hoping for a positive end to the season to boost his World Cup prospects.

England manager Thomas Tuchel will pick his squad for the tournament in the US, Mexico and Canada in May.

So far this season, Palmer has scored nine goals in 19 appearances in all competitions for the Blues.

Football.title: Man Utd player ratings: Fernandes inspires United to victory and Maguire a true leader but Yoro bullied for Palace goal
Football.content:
BRUNO FERNANDES edged closer to David Beckham’s record after leading Manchester United to a 2-1 win over 10-men Crystal Palace.

The Old Trafford skipper’s penalty and brilliant assist inspired United to come from behind after Maxence Lacroix’s early header.



Fernandes calmly levelled from the spot after Lacroix was sent off for a last man tug on Matheus Cunha shortly after half-time.



United leapfrogged Aston Villa to move third in the table after a first win over Palace at home since the 2018-2019 season.

Here’s how SunSport’s Katherine Walsh rated the United players.

Senne Lammens – 6

A little flatfooted for the opener as the Belgian did not dive but the precision of the header through bodies made it difficult to react.

Lammens did not have much to do after the break as Palace continued their form for poor second halves.



Diogo Dalot – 4

So dreadful on the ball that the Old Trafford crowd started to turn on him in the first-half.

Was more solid in the second-half as he more time on the ball as Palace’s energy dwindled. Surely this is a position United look to strengthen in the summer?

Leny Yoro – 5

The 20-year-old was at the centre of the crime for the opener when Maxence Lacroix bullied him for his long-range header. Palace targeted the Frenchman from then on.

Yoro’s lack of physicality is his Achilles heel, which makes defending aerial duels difficult. He was sloppy on the ball too, sending two long diagonal passes straight out of play.



Harry Maguire – 7

Maguire read the game well and was a true leader at the back. The 32-year-old won most of his duels and was a big presence on corners.

But it is a huge worry that Carrick was forced to replace the centre-back as he limped down the tunnel with 10 minutes remaining.

United cannot afford to have Maguire join Lisandro Martinez in the treatment room.

Luke Shaw – 5

The England full-back got the run around from Daniel Munoz and Ismaila Sarr. A Palace analyst could be heard saying there was plenty of space behind Shaw on the left.

But his streak of starting every single game for United this season is now at risk as he limped off inside 25 minutes after Munoz planted his boot on his foot in a tussle.



Casemiro – 6

Suffered to have an influence on proceedings with Adam Wharton and Sarr dominating the midfield until half-time.

Skewed a nailed on chance to equalise before the break after he headed Fernandes’ cross wide from point-blank range.

Kobbie Mainoo – 6

The 20-year-old was almost a pedestrian in the first-half as Palace controlled the tempo.

But he made more joy after the break after the red card turned the tide against a tired Palace outfit.

Bruno Fernandes – 8

You always have a chance with Fernandes on the pitch. Buried his penalty after a lengthy delay and his stunning curling cross into Sesko sent United ahead.



After spending most of the first-half barking orders at his team-mates and arguing with officials, the skipper decided to let his football do the talking.

The skipper is now two assists from matching David Beckham’s club record of 15 in a single season.

Matheus Cunha – 6

The balance on the left was off with Cunha and Noussair Mazraoui both right-footed and the Brazilian is better utilised elsewhere.

Another one guilty of losing most of his duel as he primarily struggled to handle Palace defender Chris Richards.

His theatrics for the red card gave the ref and Stockley Park lots to ponder about before Lacroix was sent off following a farcical VAR check.



Bryan Mbeumo – 6

Was left isolated again after suffering the same fate at Everton. But the Cameroon winger made several great runs.

Didn’t have the most productive of matches against Palace though, and was subbed with five minutes to play.

Benjamin Sesko – 7

There won’t be happier man in Manchester than Sesko, who headed in a classic centre forward goal superbly. It’s now six goals in seven league games for the £74m striker.

However, like so many United No9’s in recent years, the 22-year-old had so little to go at in the first-half after being starved of service.

SUBS

Noussair Mazraoui (for Shaw, 24) – 5

Did not look comfortable at left-back. His right-footedness caused problems in possession in both halves.



Amad (for Sesko, 75) – 7

After a difficult few weeks, Amad made a positive impact in the final 15 minutes. His energy was needed to get United over the line.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Ayden Heaven (for Maguire, 85) – 5

Quickly booked after coming on.

Joshua Zirkzee (for Mbeumo, 85) – 5

A few awkward moments.

Football.title: What is Chelsea’s new shirt sponsor IFS.ai?
Football.content:
CHELSEA have finally landed a front-of-shirt sponsor after going without one since the end of last season.

During the 2024/25 campaign, the Blues had no name on the front of their tops until the final weeks, when Dubai property firm DAMAC stepped in.





Chelsea chiefs were reportedly seeking £60million per year for a front-of-shirt deal in the summer after they qualified for the Champions League, won the Conference League and then lifted the Club World Cup.

But no deal came to fruition and chief revenue officer Casper Stylsvig left the club last July.

Not having a front-of-shirt sponsor for the majority of the season has meant the Blues have missed out on potentially millions in income.

Chelsea have now struck another short-term deal to help fill the void for the rest of the season.

Here’s what we know about the Blues’ new front-of-shirt sponsor IFS.

What is Chelsea’s new shirt sponsor IFS?

IFS are a Sweden-based Artificial Intelligence firm.

They are the world’s leading provider of Industrial AI software, enabling organisations to deliver results with AI by solving complex, real-world operational problems.

IFS was founded in 1983 and employs over 7,000 people in 80 countries.



The firm also sponsors both the Cadillac Formula One and the Sri Lankan national cricket teams.

When will Chelsea wear IFS on their shirts?

Liam Rosenior‘s Blues will wear IFS across their shirts for the first time on Saturday as they welcome Burnley to Stamford Bridge.

The women’s team will have the new Principal Partner on their shirts for Sunday’s FA Cup clash with Manchester United.

The overall tie-up with IFS will run until at least 2028 and Chelsea say the financial side of the agreement is in line with their expectations, while declining to reveal its value.



What’s been said?

Chelsea president Jason Gannon, who drove the deal with commercial chief Todd Kline, said: “We are incredibly proud to partner with IFS and leverage their leading edge AI software to help propel the club to even greater success.

“This partnership is a statement of intent to keep leading in this field, harnessing the opportunities advanced technology brings and unlocking the power of AI to improve everything we do on and off the pitch.”

Mark Moffat, chief executive officer of IFS, said: “In sport as in industry, the margins are small, the stakes are high, and the right decision at the right moment is everything.

“That’s what IFS Industrial AI delivers for the industries that power the global economy.

“Chelsea FC holds itself to that same uncompromising standard, and that shared ambition is exactly why we’re proud to be their Principal Partner.”

Football.title: Man Utd 2 Crystal Palace 1: Hosts go 3rd as controversial penalty sparks Fernandes-inspired comeback & Sesko nets winner
Football.content:
THEY played Magic by Pilot – the tune Manchester United fans sing Michael Carrick’s name to – for the first time at Old Trafford.

There is a good chance that the song and head coach will be permanent additions at the stadium beyond this season after another three points.





It is now six wins out of seven for unbeaten United under Carrick. They occupy third place for the first time since they finished there in May 2023.

Benjamin Sesko went from super-sub to super-starter with his third winning goal for United in only a month.

On his first start under Carrick, Sesko nodded United ahead with a heroic header to complete a swift comeback against ten-man Crystal Palace.



For 45 minutes, 3-4-2-1 worked at Old Trafford. Ruben Amorim is long gone but Oliver Glasner marshalled Palace superbly in the first half.

A cruel red card for Maxence Lacroix, with Palace 1-0 up, in the 56th minute totally changed the game. Eleven minutes later, Palace were behind.

The Eagles’ afternoon had started so promisingly. Dean Henderson, the new Palace captain, played the villain on his return to United by ordering them to attack the Stretford End in the first half.

There was maybe method to it as well. Senne Lammens was facing the sun and it possibly contributed to his statuesque goalkeeping for Lacroix’s looping header.



The sun was only out for the first ten minutes and Palace were ahead in the fourth.

Brennan Johnson’s corner stayed in the air so long that Leny Yoro did not know whether to look at the man or the ball. Lacroix brushed him off easily and guided the header in off Lammens’ right-hand post.

Yoro had a dismal day at Selhurst Park, where he did not last an hour. Jean-Philippe Mateta gave him a going-over and Palace did not need the absent Frenchman to expose Yoro again.

For the first time, Carrick became animated on the touchline, crouching down and clapping Kobbie Mainoo encouragingly after he carelessly misplaced a pass.

United fans gasped at sloppy passing from Bryan Mbeumo, Diogo Dalot and Luke Shaw while Cunha killed a promising counter-attack through poor.



One of the Palace analysts told those in the dugout about the amount of space behind Shaw, up against the tireless right wing-back Daniel Munoz.

Shaw was put out of his misery in the 24th minute when he was forced off, having been accidentally trod on by Munoz.

The United left-back limped straight down the tunnel in front of England assistant manager Anthony Barry, sitting in the directors’ box.

Henderson was intent on winding up United fans with his glacial pace at goal kicks. The Stretford End soon started booing him and he was heckled with a cry of “United reject”.

Carrick’s assistant, Steve Holland, flagged it to the fourth official as early as the 27th minute.



United did not threaten Henderson’s goal until the 31st minute when Harry Maguire met a Mbeumo corner that was blocked by Ismaila Sarr.

Their half was summed up by substitute Noussair Mazraoui and Cunha bumping into each other from Mebumo’s lay-off.

Sesko was starved of service until he directed Fernandes’ inswinging cross on target on 38 minutes.

Henderson finally made a proper save three minutes before half-time when he clawed away Fernandes’ goal-bound free-kick.

Fernandes soon created an inviting chance for Casemiro from another dead-ball kick but the Brazilian badly mistimed his header.



Despite the half-time scoreline, United fans still had a message for co-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe.

A banner unfurled above the tunnel before the second half read, ‘MUFC proudly colonised by immigrants’.

Eric Cantona, Ji-sung Park, Ole Gunnar Solskjaer, Bruno Fernandes, Amad and Casemiro adorned the banner.

Ratcliffe was not in attendance and has not been at Old Trafford for a game since Carrick’s first against Manchester City on January 17.

Palace led United 1-0 at Selhurst before fading in the reverse fixture and history repeated itself.



Lacroix’s afternoon took a turn for the worse when he tugged at Cunha’s shirt just inside the penalty area.

Referee Chris Kavanagh took a while to point to the spot and there was an even longer wait after he jogged over to the pitchside monitor to consult replays.

There was a four-minute wait between Kavanagh’s call and the red card for Lacroix, the fourth sending-off for an opponent at Old Trafford this term.

Henderson denied Tolu Arokodare of Wolves from 12 yards a week earlier but he was sent the wrong way by Fernandes for the 57th-minute equaliser.

It was then only a matter of time until a second United goal and Fernandes, still the chief creator in this attack, whipped the ball in for Sesko again.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

This time, Sesko headed the ball downwards and past the helpless Henderson.

Henderson kept Palace in it with a fine stop from Casemiro’s far-post volley on 74 minutes but United saw out the contest.

Football.title: Fulham 2 Tottenham 1: Hapless Spurs dragged deep into relegation battle as St Totteringham’s Day comes earlier than ever
Football.content:
ST TOTTERINGHAM’S DAY earlier than any other season and Spurs teetering on the edge of oblivion.

This was just the latest instalment of the Tottenham horror show this term, which looks increasingly more likely to end in relegation.





Igor Tudor’s struggling side were beaten by Fulham here thanks to first-half strikes from Harry Wilson and Alex Iwobi.

Former Arsenal star Iwobi will have particularly enjoyed his zinger for 2-0 as it ensured that Spurs can no longer mathematically finish above Arsenal this term.

The moment that unwanted fact becomes reality has been coined St Totteringham’s Day and never before has it come so early in a campaign.

Spurs stank the place out until Richarlison’s 66th-minute header threatened an unlikely comeback which never materialised.



This damaging defeat also increased Spurs’ winless Prem run to 10 games, a feat unseen since the days of Ossie Ardiles 32 years ago.

Tottenham fans, who turned their anger on the players with a chant of “You’re not fit to wear the shirt” after Iwobi’s strike, will be wondering where another win is going to come from.

Because Spurs, until their late rally, looked so woefully short of ideas in attack and open at the back, that it is very hard to see.

The only good news is their four-point gap to the drop zone was not cut thanks to West Ham’s defeat at Liverpool yesterday.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

While 17th-placed Nottingham Forest also stayed below Spurs after losing at Brighton.

The prospect of the drop seems very real for Spurs right now, so horrible is their form.

Quite how they would manage the financial black hole akin to hundreds of millions should they actually drop to the Championship is anyone’s guess.

Surely, massive earners like Cristian Romero, Xavi Simons and Conor Gallagher – all believed to be on around £200,000 a week – would have to go.



Though fans sick of the terrible displays would probably not lament their exit given the pitiful campaign so far.

Most of all the tired supporters want to see the back of the owners given the downward trend in recent years.

Those who pitched up at Craven Cottage chanted “Romero’s right, the board is s****”, “We want ENIC out” and “We want our Tottenham back”.

It is amazing to think that Spurs would have gone top of the Premier League a decade ago today (MARCH 2) had they beaten West Ham, with just ten games to go.

Now they are looking at ten final matches and wondering if the Hammers are going to switch places and plunge them below the dreaded dotted line.



This was Tudor’s second game in interim charge, having replaced the sacked Thomas Frank last month.

His first game was a disaster as arch-rivals Arsenal won 4-1 at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium.

Tudor told SunSport it was not fair to judge if his squad had enough “soldiers” for the relegation battle from that game alone, given the Gunners are “the best team in the world”.

It was not so much fight that Spurs lacked here, more quality, even with a rejigged 4-4-2 system that saw Gallagher shifted to right wing.



Spurs were not helped by a controversial call to allow Wilson’s early opener.

Raul Jimenez had appeared to push Radu Dragusin in the build-up before Wales star Wilson crashed home Oscar Bobb’s cross.

It looked very similar to the Randal Kolo Muani goal that was ruled out against Arsenal, when the Frenchman was deemed to have pushed defender Gabriel.

The difference here was that the on-field decision was ‘goal’, compared to ‘no-goal’ last week.

On both occasions the VAR decided not to overturn the call, meaning the strike stood here.



Understandably it had Spurs fans livid over the inconsistency though, with Tudor raging to fourth official James Linington on the sidelines.

Fulham supporters had no sympathy though, taunting their opponents with a chant of “Say hello to QPR”, their West London, second-tier neighbours, in response.

But as tough as that moment was to take for Tottenham, their response was meek – and Fulham took full advantage.

Just after the half hour, Iwobi strolled forward, played a neat one-two with Wilson and then let fly from 25 yards with an exquisite shot.

He hit it almost like a pass, with his instep, and it whistled in off the far post, leaving Guglielmo Vicario clutching thin air.



Footage emerged this week of Iwobi celebrating Arsenal’s goals against Spurs while on the Fulham bus coming back from Sunderland last Sunday.

Now he had a chance to enjoy a wondergoal of his own against the North Londoners.

It was at that moment the travelling fans sang ‘You’re not fit to wear the shirt’ for possibly the first time this season.

Fulham should have extended their lead further with Calvin Bassey firing over from close range just before the break and Emile Smith Rowe missing two sitters.

Tudor hooked Gallagher, Xavi Simons and Muani before the hour for Mathys Tel, Pape Matar Sarr and Richarlison.



It looked a desperate move but the changes worked as Tel worked the ball to Archie Gray, whose perfect cross was nodded home by Richarlison.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Sarr could have levelled with two minutes to go but fired into the side netting, while ex-Fulham star Joao Palhinha had an effort hacked away deep into stoppage time.

It meant the late, faint glimmer of hope turned to a sinking feeling for Tudor, that one Spurs supporters know so well.

Football.title: I left Brendan Rodgers raging after humiliating him in pool tournament… he loaned me off to Blackpool a week later
Football.content:
KIERNAN DEWSBURY-HALL left Brendan Rodgers fuming when he beat him at pool.

And the then-Leicester boss promptly sent him out on loan a week later.





Dewsbury-Hall, 27, came through the academy at his beloved boyhood club, joining aged eight.

A late bloomer, he earned his senior debut for the Foxes aged 21 under Rodgers.

But when Dewsbury-Hall schooled his manager in a pool tournament in 2020, it coincided with being shipped out to Blackpool.

The midfielder – friends with Leicester’s four-time world champion Mark Selby – told the Daily Mail: “He organised a pool tournament and everyone had to join in.

“I was playing for a pub team when I was ten years old. I mean, I can play.

“I was young at the time – wasn’t a first-team player – but was drawn to play him (Rodgers) in the first round.

“I said to the lads, ‘Do I try my hardest?’ Because he was having himself.

“He was going, ‘I’m really good at pool,’ and all the lads thought he fancied himself to win the whole thing.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

“Quite a few people were watching and I beat him 3-0 and you could tell he was raging.

“I’ve just gone, ‘Yeah, sorry gaffer,’ and a week later got sent out on loan to Blackpool. So yeah, I mean, that was a fun week of my life.”

Dewsbury-Hall thrived at Blackpool, and then Luton, before becoming a mainstay at the King Power as Rodgers let the pool incident slide.

But after following Enzo Maresca to Chelsea for £30million, things did not pan out as planned.

He impressed in the Conference League run to glory but made just two Premier League starts.



The ex-Leicester man described his year at Stamford Bridge as “dark times” where his “rival midfielders were £100million players”.

Leaving for Everton last summer has reinvigorated his career, stepping up as David Moyes’ main man, especially with Jack Grealish’s injury.

And his fine form – five goals and five assists – could see him squeeze his way into Thomas Tuchel’s World Cup squad.

That could see him spend six weeks away from home just months after his baby daughter, due imminently, arrives.

Uncapped Dewbsury-Hall added on Tuchel and his international prospects: “I’ve spoken to him once or twice.



“He was at Old Trafford when I scored. He was at Fulham when I scored. So I wouldn’t mind him coming to more games!

“He’s been complimentary and told me to stick at it. I feel like I am ready now. I feel like I am moving forwards again.

“It almost felt like no matter how well I played in midweek [at Chelsea], I would not play at the weekend and that was the only thing that didn’t sit well with me.

“In football it’s important, if you’re playing well, that you get the chance to play.

“I’m a very emotional guy. If I’m not playing, I feel useless, basically. I feel like I haven’t got a purpose because that’s my life.



“I am not gonna cry over it. I know if I did play more I would have been able to show people what I could do. In my head, that’s a fact.”

But Dewsbury-Hall would one day love to swap the football stage for the theatre stage.

The keen musical fanatic almost featured in a BBC production of The Lion King and has dreams of performing in the West End.

He has struggled to convince team-mates to get into it quite as much, though.

Dewsbury-Hall continued: “They just look at me like I am alien.



“But one day I genuinely believe I’ll do something in the West End.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“I resonate with what they do every night, giving their best to a completely new set of eyes.

“That’s hard to do, so I respect them so much.”

Football.title: Real Madrid star Kylian Mbappe, 27, spotted finally taking driving lessons to get behind wheel of his £2m supercar fleet
Football.content:
KYLIAN MBAPPE has been spotted getting driving lessons at a neighbourhood driving school in Spain.

The superstar French forward, 27, owns a £2million supercar fleet, but has NEVER been able to drive one by himself.





Fans saw Mbappe climb into a training car, which costs as much as his tyres on his brand new BMW i7, in the city of Alcorcon south of Madrid.

Coincidentally, his teacher is reportedly an Atletico Madrid fan.

His instructor said: “I’m very much an Atletico fan, but Kylian couldn’t have been more easy-going.”

Many past and present Atletico players had picked up their licenses from the very same place.



Lara Driving School has since been inundated with phone calls and fan requests to leave their jerseys for Mbappe to sign when he returns.

It is now next to impossible for the Real Madrid star to continue his lessons without being swarmed by people.

Fans are also surprised that Mbappe, who turns 28 this year, has never owned a driver’s license.

One wrote on X: “I mean if he doesn’t have it yet, sure why not. But most people get it right after they turn 18 right.”



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Another said: “It is hilarious to think that a guy who can outrun a professional defender at 35 km/h is only just now learning how to drive a car.”

Mbappe has to be chauffeured around in the cars due to his failure to get the licence during a manic time in his life as he shot to stardom.

He spoke about his lack of a driving licence back in 2023 and said it simply wasn’t a priority for him during his career.



Mbappe said: “It’s one of the downsides of being successful early, I missed simple things like having a driver’s license.

“I think everyone has one. For many people a license is an obligation, but it wasn’t for me.

“Although it is synonymous with independence, I had my independence early on because I had drivers at my disposal. It has never been a priority.”

Mbappe has had another strong year in La Liga, netting 23 goals in as many appearances this season.

He is on track to win his second Pichichi Trophy with Madrid.

Football.title: Arsenal fans celebrate earliest ever St Totteringham's Day after Spurs' defeat at Fulham
Football.content:
Arsenal fans celebrate earliest ever St Totteringham's Day after Spurs' defeat at Fulham

Arsenal are now guaranteed to finish above North London rivals Tottenham Hotspur after Igor Tudor’s men slipped to a dismal defeat away from home against Fulham

View 2 Images Arsenal are now guaranteed to finish above Spurs (Image: Getty Images)

Arsenal fans already have reason to celebrate this season, having marked St Totteringham’s Day following Spurs’ defeat against Fulham. Igor Tudor’s men slipped to a 2-1 loss at Craven Cottage on Sunday afternoon.

‌



Harry Wilson opened the scoring for the home side, with former Arsenal man Alex Iwobi doubling the advantage. Richarlison found a foothold for Spurs in the second-half but they were unable to complete the turnaround.

‌



And while Arsenal will have been toasting another miserable afternoon for their bitter rivals, their defeat has taken on even more significance.

‌



With just 10 games and 30 points left to play for this season, Spurs already find themselves 32 points behind Arsenal, meaning that they can no longer officially finish above them in the Premier League table - an occasion that has affectionately been dubbed St Totteringham’s Day by Arsenal fans.

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

The day however is even more significant this season, having arrived earlier than any other campaign. Arsenal fans took to social media to celebrate the occasion.

‌



One fan said: “Once a Gooner, always a Gooner. Happy St Totteringham's Day Iwobi!,” while another added: “Happy St. Totteringham's Day..”

Arsenal have been dominant over their North London rivals in the Premier League era. Arsenal have finished above their fierce rivals in 25 of the past 33 Premier League seasons - a run that has now been extended.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



View 2 Images Spurs sunk to another miserable defeat against Fulham (Image: Getty Images)

And while the Premier League title might be slightly higher on their agenda, they could potentially secure another memorable record against Tottenham before the end of the campaign.

The biggest gap between the two sides at the end of a season has been 45 points, with Arsenal amassing 90 points on their way to the title during the 2003/04 campaign - a season in which they finished the season without losing a game.

‌



Arsenal took a significant step towards beating that record by beating Spurs last week away from home. Eberechi Eze netted a brace against Spurs, having previously netted a hat-trick against them earlier this season.

Randal Kolo-Miani had managed to find the net for Tudor’s side to level matters. But Viktor Gyokeres netted a second-half brace to round off a comprehensive win for Arsenal.

That win also saw Arsenal take a significant step towards the Premier League title. But Spurs remain in real relegation trouble, with the side having just a four point advantage over 18th-placed West Ham.

Football.title: Benjamin Sesko scores again as Man Utd punish Crystal Palace red card - 5 talking points
Football.content:
Benjamin Sesko scores again as Man Utd punish Crystal Palace red card - 5 talking points

MAN UTD 2-1 CRYSTAL PALACE: In-form Benjamin Sesko scored the winning goal for the second game running, as Manchester United came from behind to beat Oliver Glasner's team at Old Trafford

View 5 Images Maxence Lacroix's red card proved a turning point (Image: Richard Sellers/Sportsphoto/Allstar via Getty Images)

Benjamin Sesko was the hero for Manchester United yet again, as Michael Carrick's side came from behind to beat Crystal Palace 2-1. The result moves United up to third in the Premier League table, on course to quality for the Champions League.

‌



Maxence Lacroix opened the scoring with just minutes on the clock, punishing some poor defending from United. The Red Devils offered little in the way of a response, with Casemiro spurning their best chance of a lethargic first half.

‌



But Carrick's men were much improved in the second period. Lacroix gave them a leg up by going from hero to zero, getting himself sent off and giving United a penalty which Bruno Fernandes duly dispatched.

‌



Less than 10 minutes later, Sesko rose highest to meet a Fernandes cross and continued his hot streak in front of goal. Palace pushed for an unlikely equaliser, but the Red Devils saw out the win and made it nine league games unbeaten.

Here are five talking points from Old Trafford.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



1. No hangover for Palace

Given Palace had played a Europa League knockout game on Thursday, you'd have forgiven them for making a slow start. But the Eagles got off to a flier thanks to Lacroix's looping header in the fourth minute.

Brennan Johnson hoisted in a corner and the Palace centre-back managed to evade his marker, Leny Yoro. Lacroix made good contact and with no one on United's front post, the ball nestled in and Senne Lammens' chances of back-to-back clean sheets.

BACK THE MIRROR! Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search

‌



View 5 Images Maxence Lacroix opened the scoring on four minutes (Image: Darren Staples/AFP via Getty Images)

2. Shaw's injury woes return

Few players in the Premier League have suffered as much as Luke Shaw when it comes to injuries. This season, however, Shaw has started all 28 of United's top-flight games, his longest streak since joining the Red Devils in 2014.

Unfortunately, that run likely to come to an end when United travel to Newcastle on Wednesday. The 30-year-old came off worse in a challenge with Daniel Munoz and went down when the ball next went out of play.

‌



Shaw immediately looked to be coming off, and rather than receive treatment, he sat down and talked to a member of United's medical staff while Noussair Mazraoui prepared to come on. The blow comes at a frustrating time for Shaw, who'd been tipped for an England call-up later this month.

View 5 Images Luke Shaw came off injured in the first half (Image: Stu Forster/Getty Images)

3. Lacroix goes from hero to zero

United came out for the second half looking much sharper and soon enough their intent was rewarded. Matheus Cunha got a run on Lacroix and the Frenchman grabbed hold of the United forward's shoulder as they raced into the penalty area.

‌



Cunha went bundling over and Chris Kavanagh pointed to the spot. The referee initially didn't show Lacroix a card but was soon sent over to the VAR monitor to decide his fate.

After a few replays showing that Lacroix had denied Cunha a clear goalscoring opportunity, Kavanagh announced to the stadium that the Palace offender would be going for an early bath. Fernandes stepped up to take the penalty and sent Dean Henderson the wrong way, levelling the scores.

View 5 Images Maxence Lacroix was adjudged to have fouled Matheus Cunha (Image: Molly Darlington/Copa/Getty Images)

‌



4. Sesko strikes again

Handed a start by Carrick for the first time, Sesko didn't let his boss down after a frustrating hour with little to feed off. Some sustained United pressure allowed Fernandes to whip in a sumptuous cross towards the Slovenia international, who took flight and planted his header past the helpless Henderson.

It's now four goals in five games for Sesko, putting his slow start to life at Old Trafford behind him. The Red Devils had completed the comeback and given 10-man Palace a mountain to climb.

View 5 Images Benjamin Sesko is in red-hot form (Image: Darren Staples/AFP via Getty Images)

‌



5. Carrick's home comforts

It's now four wins from four home games as United manager for Carrick. Since taking the wheel, the Red Devils have beaten Manchester City, Fulham, Tottenham and now Palace at Old Trafford.

It was far from a vintage display, with United's first-half performance echoing their sluggish start against Everton. Like at Hill Dickinson Stadium, though, they improved after the break and took advantage of Aston Villa slipping up against relegation-threatened Wolves to go third.

Football.title: Man Utd penalty and red card vs Crystal Palace explained as VAR intervenes
Football.content:
Man Utd penalty and red card vs Crystal Palace explained as VAR intervenes

Manchester United were handed a route back into their Premier League clash with Crystal Palace when Maxence Lacroix was sent off and Bruno Fernandes scored the penalty

View 2 Images Maxence Lacroiz was sent off for Crysta; Palace against Man United. (Image: Alex Livesey, Getty Images)

Manchester United were given a lifeline in their Premier League match on Sunday thanks to a contentious penalty and red card against Crystal Palace.

‌



Michael Carrick's side found themselves trailing early at Old Trafford when Maxence Lacroix's arcing header got the better of Senne Lammens. The rest of the first half saw United frustrated by the Eagles, but they were given a chance to take control of the game in the second half.

‌



Bruno Fernandes' pass set Matheus Cunha up for a shot on goal, only for Lacroix to pull the Brazilian down inside the box. Referee Chris Kavanagh signalled for a penalty.

‌



FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Moments later, he was directed to the on-field VAR screen before declaring that the Palace player would be dismissed.

"After review, Crystal Palace number 5 commits a clear holding offence which denies a clear, obvious goal scoring opportunity," Kavanagh said.

‌



"Final decision is penalty and red card."

Fernandes slotted home the penalty to equalise. Benjamin Sesko then put United in front from Fernandes' superb cross to extend his impressive scoring streak and complete the turnaround for United.

View 2 Images Benjamin Sesko scored the winning goal for Manchester United against Crystal Palace. (Image: Richard Sellers/Sportsphoto/Allstar via Getty Images)

‌



Lacroix's heavy involvement in the game followed doubt about being able to start for Palace, after making a goalscoring return from injury during their UEFA Conference League match on Thursday night.

While the Eagles were in European action, United looked to make the most of having extra time to prepare for the Premier League meeting.

With the numerical advantage, as well as the extra rest, United were able to see out the game to continue their impressive run under the stewardship of Michael Carrick. The win is the team's sixth in seven matches since the interim manager took charge.

‌



Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Following Aston Villa's loss against Wolves on Friday night, the three points see United climb above the Midlands side on goal difference and into third place in the Premier League table.

United don't have too long to wait until the next game comes around with them back in action on Wednesday when they travel to St James' Park to take on Newcastle United.

Article continues below

Football.title: Luke Littler banned from playing in event as darts star's public plea shut down
Football.content:
Luke Littler banned from playing in event as darts star's public plea shut down

Luke Littler was publicly turned down after requesting to play in an event in front of a sold-out crowd

View 2 Images Luke Littler won't be featuring at a star-studded event in April (Image: Stu Forster, Getty Images)

Luke Littler's bid to participate in the Sidemen Charity Match was unequivocally rejected by YouTube sensation KSI. The annual showdown featuring content creators and influencers is fast approaching, with the 2026 edition scheduled for April 18th at a sold-out Wembley Stadium.

‌



A favourite amongst social media enthusiasts, last year's match at the national stadium saw all seven members of the Sidemen face off against the YouTube Allstars, with personalities such as AngryGinge, IShowSpeed, Logan Paul and Mr Beast all participating in the game.

‌



While this year's line-up remains unconfirmed, the reigning darts world champion expressed his interest. In early February, the 19-year-old reposted an Instagram post by the Sidemen, who had announced to their 6.2million followers that tickets for the annual match sold out within hours.

‌



MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

Tagging the group, Littler wrote: "Get me in the team Sidemen," sparking excitement among fans at the prospect of seeing the Manchester United supporter try his luck at football.

Luke Littler showed true colours after his behaviour was called out by darts rival READ MORE :

Darts fans angered after Sky Sports mistake during Premier League action READ MORE :

However, the likelihood of that happening was definitively quashed by KSI during the 32-year-old's appearance on Capital FM this week. When asked if he had responded to Littler's request, the Britain's Got Talent judge simply replied: "I have not responded, and he's not in."

‌



He did clarify that the refusal of Littler is due to one rule the Sidemen have always adhered to for the game. KSI elaborated: "It sounds so bad, but we only have content creators on the Sidemen Charity Match.

View 2 Images KSI said the reigning darts world champion is not allowed to play in the Sidemen Charity Match (Image: Getty)

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



"So we haven't ever had a celebrity come on to play. That's more for like Soccer Aid. You never know, maybe one day we'll have Soccer Aid versus Sidemen."

Insisting that snubbing Littler was not personal, KSI conceded that even the most famous celebrities in the world could request to participate and the response would still be a firm no.

He added: "I think for us we're focusing more on just content creators. That's how it's always been, and even if Justin Bieber wanted to play, we'd go, 'No! Content creators only!'"

Article continues below

The match is set to commence at 3pm and will be broadcast live on the Sidemen's YouTube channel. It will generate funds for charities Bright Side and M7 Education - the latter being established by Sidemen member Simon 'Miniminter' Minter and his brother Nick.

Football.title: Inside Rio Ferdinand's new life in Dubai after quitting UK as warning issued to Brits
Football.content:
Inside Rio Ferdinand's new life in Dubai after quitting UK as warning issued to Brits

Rio Ferdinand left the UK for Dubai in 2025 after working for a decade as a pundit on UK TV following his professional playing career

View 5 Images Rio and Kate Ferdinand moved their young family to Dubai (Image: rioferdy5/Instagram)

Rio Ferdinand departed the UK to move to Dubai with his family after stepping down from his role as a TNT Sports pundit. Fans will be keeping an eye on the Manchester United icon's whereabouts following the UK government's warning to Brits in the United Arab Emirates.

‌



A wave of missile attacks has thrown the city into turmoil with UK citizens told to remain safe indoors. The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development website said: "Due to reported missile attacks, British nationals in UAE should immediately shelter in place.

‌



"Remain indoors in a secure location, avoid all travel and follow instructions from the local authorities. There is a heightened risk of regional tension. Escalation could lead to travel disruption and other unanticipated impacts. British nationals should take sensible precautions, considering their own individual circumstances."

‌



It is unconfirmed whether Ferdinand and his family are currently in Dubai at present. However, they moved to the region after the ex-United defender announced he would step down from TNT Sports.

Rio Ferdinand's huge net worth and new life in Dubai with TV star wife READ MORE :

In a statement, the 47-year-old said: "I want to acknowledge the tremendous support from the team behind the scenes whose hard work often goes unseen but has been essential to our success.

Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

‌



"To everyone at TNT Sports, we have a brilliant end to the season to look forward to on Saturday and for now all focus will be on what I think could go down as one of the great European finals. The network continues to thrive with more rights than ever. I know I look forward to following its progress and the exciting future that lies ahead.

"Lastly I would like to thank my family and my management company New Era for their ongoing support. They have supported me to travel all over Europe following the best players and watching the best matches."

5 View 5 Images The pair decided to make the big life decision in 2025 (Image: xkateferdinand/Instagram) 5 View 5 Images Ferdinand's older children have remained in the UK (Image: rioferdy5/Instagram)

‌



It was reported last year that the Ferdinand family had been scoping out homes and schools in Dubai and decided to make the move. Ferdinand shares two children with wife Kate, who is also a stepmother to his three kids from his previous marriage to Rebecca Ellison - who passed away from cancer in 2015.

However, Ferdinand's older kids elected to remain in the UK amid their 2025 move. Ferdinand stated there wasn't a stand-out reason for the Dubai move. He told The National: "There’s not one reason.

"I went for the Global Soccer Awards at Christmas and I hadn’t been there properly for about seven or eight years. We spent time with some of Kate’s friends who live there and I just saw a different side to it. I also have a business out there, Football Escapes."

‌



5 View 5 Images The pair were looking forward to life in Dubai (Image: xkateferdinand/Instagram) 5 View 5 Images Kate has admitted she feels 'homesick' (Image: rioferdy5/Instagram)

Touching on the family-led environment he was touched by, Ferdinand added: "In Dubai, it feels like they’re valued and the lifestyle means that they’re happy and vibrant with a good energy for the kids. Lifestyle, safety, weather — it’s just a new life.

‌



"When I made the decision, I thought, ‘This must be 30 years when my life has been driven by the fixture list. Not now.’ I’m not leaving football, but it’s just not the governing body of my life now. I’ve got to be honest, it feels nice."

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Wife Kate was also upbeat about the move, adding that she was nervous for the huge life decision. The former The Only Way is Essex star said: "A new chapter, a fresh start - because if we don't try, we'll never know.

Article continues below

"Ahhh! We've talked about this for so long, and now it's finally real... we're here we are making a new home in the place we met! Missing some of our specials already and a bit scared, excited and nervous - but more than anything, ready for fresh starts and new beginnings."

However, Kate has admitted to feeling homesick during an episode of her Blended podcast. She said: "Rio loves it so much. I am enjoying it, but I miss home quite a lot. I get upset. I miss the big boys a lot and I'm just struggling with that."

Football.title: Mikel Arteta told Arsenal duo can win them the title - but he 'doesn't trust' them
Football.content:
Mikel Arteta told Arsenal duo can win them the title - but he 'doesn't trust' them

Mikel Arteta has been questioned over the trust he has in two Arsenal stars in the Premier League title race, with Manchester City putting pressure on the Gunners

View 2 Images Mikel Arteta has been questioned over his trust of two Arsenal player (Image: (Photo by Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images))

Former Arsenal star Paul Merson has questioned whether Mikel Arteta fully trusts Eberechi Eze and Viktor Gyokeres to deliver in crucial moments for the team during their Premier League title challenge.

‌



It comes as the Gunners are facing mounting pressure from Pep Guardiola and Manchester City, having witnessed their advantage at the summit drastically reduced in recent weeks. Disappointing stalemates against Wolves and Brentford, alongside a loss to Manchester United, have all affected the north London outfit, with the title race now wide open.

‌



With a demanding fixture list approaching, which includes a London derby against rivals Chelsea, it'll be the moment for numerous pivotal Arsenal players to step forward and deliver. That will also hinge on how they are handled by Arteta, with certain stars requiring confidence to feature on the pitch and make a difference.

‌



Arsenal news: Brazilian starlet issues transfer plea as Mikel Arteta urged to make bold call READ MORE :

Two of those are summer acquisitions Eze and Gyokeres, who have both enjoyed promising spells, but are seeking some regularity in their displays. Merson believes that the duo thrive from playing alongside one another, as he said on Sky Sports' Soccer Saturday: "Yeah, he put Saka in there against Wolves. It's a hard position to play, the ten.

"You don't just put someone in there and go 'oh you do what you want and make something happen'. You've got to find pockets and Eze is very good at that. I think that's what he was bought for, to get in there those little pockets; if you get him on the ball he can turn, he's elegant, he goes past people like they're not there.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



"That game [North London Derby] was perfect for Arteta - they win the game 4-1 and Gyokeres gets two goals and Eze scores two. I mean, if you could pick two players to get the goals, those would be it. If these two hit fire until the end of the season, Arsenal win the league. If they can go and be 8/10 every week.

"Because we said at the start, when Eze got bought: he was the X-factor and going to be the one who got them over the line, I still think that's the case as long as he plays and Arteta trusts him."

Merson then questioned whether the manager genuinely trusts the duo, as he went on to say: "No, I don't think he does a lot of people. I think Arteta plays on sparks where he'll watch someone do something wrong and 'oh you're out of the team'.

‌



"We saw it with Calafiori not too long ago; he had a nightmare game, gets ripped off, don't play again. Eze gets caught on the far post against Cash at Villa, don't really play again. You've got to give them a chance, trust in them more, these players, I don't think he does that enough in my opinion."

View 2 Images Viktor Gyokeres and Eberechi Eze were crucial for Arsenal against Tottenha (Image: (Photo by Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images))

It remains uncertain precisely how much confidence Arteta will invest in Eze and Gyokeres, who will be hoping that their showings in the North London Derby can propel them towards fresh heights. For Eze, the encounter was another demonstration of his capacity to appear at the crucial moment to provide a finishing flourish to an attack.

‌



Given his hat-trick against Tottenham in the reverse fixture, it's evident that he possesses the ability to deliver a crucial moment. The question now is whether he can reproduce this repeatedly when it truly counts in the Premier League title race, with every point now vital to their challenge at the summit.

As for Gyokeres, numerous observers have suggested that his showing against Spurs was his finest yet in an Arsenal shirt. Not only were there goals to celebrate, but the comprehensive link-up play left many impressed with the Swedish international, optimistic that he can now mirror that performance going forward.

"Well, I think one thing leads to another," Arteta said on Gyokeres' form. "When you score the first one, or the performance is good, you have more time with your teammates, you understand the games, the opponents, the league better, everything helps.

Article continues below

"We know his qualities, he's undoubtedly an incredible striker. We need to feed into his quality, we need to understand him better, he needs to understand the team and the league better, and I think we are on the right trajectory."

If that trajectory proves correct for the summer additions, then they will assist in propelling Arsenal towards the finish line this season.

Football.title: Lamine Yamal makes blunder as he PASSES to Barcelona boss and admits he 'wasn't happy'
Football.content:
Lamine Yamal makes blunder as he PASSES to Barcelona boss and admits he 'wasn't happy'

Lamine Yamal was on hand to score a first hat-trick during Barcelona's thumping win over Villarreal - but the teenager endured a rare blunder as he passed to his manager

View 2 Images Lamine Yamal passed to Hansi Flick during a Barcelona counter attack

Lamine Yamal made a comical error during Barcelona's victory over Villarreal as he passed to manager Hansi Flick which ended a counter attack.

‌



The Spanish sensation scored a hat-trick as the Catalans continued their pursuit of another La Liga crown, but made a blunder which left him and his German boss smiling. Barcelona were leading 1-0 when their attack was curtailed by Yamal playing a pass to Flick.

‌



The teenager more than made up for it, scoring the first treble of his career at the Nou Camp - he became the first 18-year-old to net a treble in the Spanish top flight since 1967. Yamal has now scored 18 times this term, making him the team's top scorer.

‌



Despite his impressive exploits Yamal concedes he's struggled to get going this term, before adding that he now feels better heading into the final stretch of the season.

Yamal told Movistar: "I didn't feel great [earlier this season]. There was the [groin] problem and, generally, I wasn't happy. It was a mix of many things. You could tell. I have felt a lot better for a week or so now. I am smiling when I am playing, which wasn't the case for a while, and I am happy on the pitch."

MAKE THE MIRROR YOUR FIRST CHOICE! Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

‌



The Barcelona sensation, who narrowly missed out on the Ballon d'Or last term, has looked to put some perspective on his numbers. "I'm scoring more goals all the time," he added. "At 16 people already want you to score 100 goals already and it's difficult. ... I want to score more as well, but it's not always possible."

Yamal has already matched the goal tally he posted last season as he continues to grow in stature at the Nou Camp. Flick has claimed that his teenage star is being ganged up on by opponents as his influence on games keeps increasing.

View 2 Images The teenager scored his first career hat-trick on Saturday

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

"The opponent sometimes they play two or three players against him," Flick said. "You could feel he had not the confidence at times, the dynamic for this one against one situation. I am happy for today, it has to give him confidence, and when he enjoys playing, it's perfect for him and also for us."

Barcelona are seeking to become the first Spanish champions since 2019 to retain their crown with the Catalans, Real Madrid and Atletico Madrid all claiming the title in the past six seasons.

Football.title: Jose Mourinho breaks silence after criticism over Vinicius Junior incident with Benfica vow
Football.content:
Jose Mourinho breaks silence after criticism over Vinicius Junior incident with Benfica vow

Jose Mourinho was heavily criticised after accusing Vinicius Junior of inciting the Benfica crowd amid allegations that the Real Madrid star was on the receiving end of a racial slur from Gianluca Prestianni

View 2 Images Jose Mourinho was criticised over comments on Real Madrid star Vinicius Junior (Image: UEFA via Getty Images)

Benfica manager Jose Mourinho has insisted he will not look at Gianluca Prestianni "in the same way" if the Argentine is found guilty of directing a racial slur towards Vinicius Junior. Benfica winger Prestianni was accused of directing the slur at the Real Madrid star during their Champions League playoff first-leg fixture on February 17.

‌



The confrontation between them led to a 10-minute delay in play as referee Francois Letexier initiated UEFA's formal anti-racism protocol. The European footballing governing body handed Prestianni a provisional one-match suspension while an ethics and disciplinary inspector investigates the allegations further. He denies racially abusing the Brazilian.

‌



It meant the 26-year-old was suspended for the second leg of their Champions League tie against Real on Wednesday night. The Spanish giants won 2-1 at the Santiago Bernabeu to progress 3-1 on aggregate.

‌



Mourinho was heavily criticised after accusing Vinicius Jr of inciting the crowd with his goal celebration before the alleged racial slur in the first leg. And he has now broken his silence on the criticism in a pre-match press conference before Benfica's league fixture at Gil Vicente on Monday night.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

He said: "If he is indeed guilty, I will no longer look at him the same way, and it will be over with me. I absolutely condemn all forms of discrimination and idiocy.

‌



"If my player has not respected the principles, which are mine and those of Benfica, his career will end with me."

View 2 Images Vinicius Jr accused Gianluca Prestianni of a racist comment (Image: Getty Images)

Benfica have also denied claims that Prestianni admitted to his team-mates that he had racially abused Vinicius Jr.

‌



**Ensure our latest sport headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. **Click here to activate or add us as Preferred Source in your Google search settings

In a statement, they said: "Sport Lisboa e Benfica categorically denies that the player Prestianni has communicated to the squad or the club's structure that he has uttered a racist insult to the player Vinicius Jr, from Real Madrid.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

Article continues below

"As has already been made public, the player apologised to his teammates for the incident that occurred during the match against Real Madrid, regretting the dimension and consequences of it and assuring everyone, as he has done since the first hour, that he is not racist."

Prestianni has publicly denied the allegations saying his opponent had "misinterpreted". He could be banned for 10 matches if found guilty.

Football.title: Man Utd into top three as Sesko completes turnaround against 10-man Palace
Football.content:
Benjamin Sesko celebrated his first start under Michael Carrick with the winner as Man Utd came from behind to beat 10-man Crystal Palace 2-1 and move third for the first time since May 2023.

Sesko's header from Bruno Fernandes' cross was the Slovenian's fourth goal in five games but the encounter ultimately swung on the earlier dismissal of Palace defender Maxence Lacroix 10 minutes into the second half.

The Frenchman was adjudged to have pulled Matheus Cunha back as he entered the box and Chris Kavanagh pointed to the spot after an initial hesitation, before the subsequent VAR review compounded the Eagles' misery when Lacroix was sent off for denying a clear goalscoring opportunity - before Fernandes pulled the hosts level from the spot.

Palace had dominated much of the game up to Fernandes' penalty after taking a fourth-minute lead through Lacroix himself, and though Man Utd had returned from the break the better side they had not tested goalkeeper Dean Henderson before Kavanagh's intervention.

But with that man advantage, they looked certain to continue their upward momentum under Carrick and duly completed their turnaround only eight minutes after their equaliser through Sesko's fine header.

While extending their unbeaten Premier League run to 11 games Man Utd also lifted themselves back up to third in the table above Aston Villa, with Crystal Palace falling to 15th as Brighton jumped above them by beating Nottingham Forest.

Story of the match in stats

More to follow...

Football.title: Spurs' relegation fears deepen with loss at Fulham
Football.content:
Tottenham’s relegation fears deepened after a 2-1 defeat at Fulham equalled their longest Premier League winless run.

Spurs substitute Richarlison gave the visitors hope of a second-half comeback, but first-half goals from Harry Wilson and Alex Iwobi meant Tottenham are now without victory in 10 top-flight games.

Spurs, who are winless in 2026, remain 16th and just four points above the drop zone with 10 games left.

Spurs' loss in west London highlights the huge job new head coach Igor Tudor has on his hands as he became just the second Tottenham boss to lose his first two Premier League games in charge.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Alex Iwobi and Harry Wilson combine to score a fabulous Fulham goal against Spurs in the Premier League

But Fulham are heading in the opposite direction under Marco Silva, with the Cottagers firmly in the hunt for a European spot after back-to-back wins put them ninth.

Fulham should have won more comfortably after Emile Smith Rowe missed two glorious second-half chances, but they survived a nervy ending set up by Richarlison's 66th-minute header, which was Spurs' only shot on target of the game.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Richarlison pulls one back for Spurs against Fulham in the Premier League

Tudor went 4-4-2 in west London, but Fulham immediately preyed on Tottenham's vulnerability as Wilson struck a seventh-minute opener.

Spurs were unhappy with the goal as they felt Radu Dragusin was pushed in the back by Fulham striker Raul Jimenez in the build-up to the goal, but VAR deemed the contact "did not meet the threshold for a foul".

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Wilson hammers home an early goal against Spurs despite Jimenez's push on Dragusin in the build-up

Tottenham's nightmare first half, in which they failed to have a shot on target, worsened when Iwobi hit a superb 34th-minute goal as he arrowed a ball from distance into the bottom far corner off the post.

Spurs substitutes Mathys Tel and Richarlison sparked a second-half improvement from Tudor's side as the former set Archie Gray up for his cross that led to Richarlison's goal.

But Tottenham's wretched form continues, having now won only two of their last 19 Premier League matches.

Team news Tottenham made two changes from last weekend's home defeat to Arsenal as Pedro Porro and Dominic Solanke started.

Porro returned from a hamstring injury to replace the absent Djed Spence, while Pape Sarr was benched.

Fulham made two changes from the win at Sunderland, as Issa Diop took the place of the absent Joachim Andersen, while injured Kevin was replaced by Oscar Bobb, who made his first Premier League start for the club.

Story of the match in stats

Football.title: Röhl still believes in Rangers' title bid but Boyd backs Hearts
Football.content:
A defiant Danny Röhl believes Rangers are not out of the Scottish Premiership title race, but Sky Sports' Kris Boyd is now backing Hearts for the top spot.

Rangers blew a two-goal lead against rivals Celtic on Sunday to draw 2-2 in the derby, a result which has left them six points behind leaders Hearts with nine games remaining, while Celtic's comeback means they are eight behind with a game in hand.

Röhl's side have won just three of their last six league matches which has seen their title bid stall, although the Rangers head coach reckons they are not out of it yet.

"Anyone who has played football knows during a game there is momentum," he said after Celtic's second-half comeback.

"It's about continuing our performance over 95 minutes. If we do this, they would not have a chance to come back.

"We have a six-point gap with nine games to go. I will say again and again, it's about the final matchday and not today. We will fight for every point until the end.

"Nine games is massive. I totally believe in my players."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Dujon Sterling's handball gifts Celtic late penalty that Reo Hatate puts away to level the score in the Old Firm.

However, Sky Sports' Boyd, who had been backing Rangers for the title, has changed his mind after the result at Ibrox.

"Hearts have been at the top of the table since October. It is no fluke, it's not a purple patch. They have been outstanding," he said.

"I was saying Rangers [are favourites] last week, I've got to say now that I think Hearts are the team for me. Rangers' form, it's three wins in eight in all competitions. That is not championship-winning form.

"Celtic have been inconsistent as well, Motherwell have been outstanding, might have just given themselves too much to get back at Hearts.

"Hearts have two home games, one of them against Motherwell, two games against the bottom two in the league away from home. I am swaying more towards Hearts now."

'We're not out of it'

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Martin O'Neill reflects on Celtic's 2-2 draw in the Old Firm and believes his side are still very much in the title race.

Celtic's game in hand is on Wednesday at Aberdeen, live on Sky Sports, and boss Martin O'Neill still fancies their chances in the title race.

"If you belong to Hearts, you'd think that's a really good result for them, but even so we're not out of it," he said after the Old Firm draw.

"If the game had ended at half-time and after our performance in the first half, you'd have thought we're a million miles off it.

"Now at the end of the game, in the manner we played, particularly at Ibrox, 2-0 down, to fight back, there's still plenty of heart and desire to try and retain the championship.

"It's been a really tough old struggle this season and we just have to dig it out. To still be in the title race, it looked pretty daunting at half-time."

'Draw will feel like a defeat for Rangers'

Sky Sports pundit Chris Sutton praised O'Neill for his decisive half-time changes - introducing Reo Hatate and Sebastian Tounekti - which sparked the comeback, and said he would deserve all the credit if Celtic go on to win the title.

"For Rangers to be two up and let that slip, it will feel like a defeat. Martin O'Neill will be happy they got a point and maybe with a bit more care at the end, could have got three.

"What a strange game. It sums up both teams pretty well. Celtic were bullied in the first half. Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain couldn't get to the pace of the game in midfield. It was like he was thinking it was a testimonial.

"The changes from Martin O'Neill were really decisive. However bad Reo Hatate has been this season and has been sulking for part of the season, but he showed what a quality player he can be and actually is. He dictated the second half.

"Celtic were the dominant team - 16 of the 17 shots they had were in the second half. Martin O'Neill will be delighted with that recovery. A point suits Hearts better than everyone."

Football.title: Hatate rescues Celtic in Old Firm classic
Football.content:
Reo Hatate scored a late equaliser to rescue a point for Celtic as they came from two goals down to draw 2-2 with Rangers in a pulsating Old Firm clash at Ibrox.

A Youssef Chermiti wonder goal put Rangers ahead early on as the hosts piled on the pressure before the striker took advantage of some slack Celtic defending to double their lead before the half-hour mark.

Celtic improved tremendously after half-time with Kieran Tierney's header reducing the deficit and they were the better team in the second period.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Rangers striker Youssef Chermiti scored an incredible overhead kick similar to the famous goal by Wayne Rooney for Manchester United

They got their reward in the dying minutes when Daizen Maeda's header struck the hand of Dujon Sterling, with Hatate's spot-kick and follow up initially saved by Jack Butland before the midfielder eventually bundled the ball into the net.

Both sides had watched Hearts extend their lead at the top the day before and knew a win on either side was vital to keep the pressure on the league leaders in an enthralling title race.

The game started at its usual frantic tempo but it was the home side who settled with more purpose. Nico Raskin forced Viljami Sinisalo into an early stop before being called back for offside but it was a sign of building Rangers pressure.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Youssef Chermiti capitalises on Dane Murray's error at the back to get his second goal in the Old Firm

They did take the lead just eight minutes in with Andreas Skov Olsen standing his cross up to the centre of the box where Chermiti met it with a stunning overhead kick to fire the ball past Sinisalo.

The hosts had their tails up and nearly got a second soon after. Mikey Moore advanced with pace down the left before standing a ball up to the back post but Chermiti and Skov Olsen combined to knock the ball onto the outside of the post and out for a goal kick.

Celtic did try to work their way back into the game with Daizen Maeda's cross evading Junior Adamu before Tierney called Butland into action with a dangerous low ball into the box.

Rangers looked dangerous every time they got into Celtic's final third, however, and they doubled their lead after 26 minutes. Dane Murray made a mess of trying to clear Raskin's clipped ball over the top, with a persistent Chermiti then robbing the ball from the path of Julian Araujo to flick the ball round the defender and finish past Sinisalo.

Image: Celtic's Kieran Tierney scores to make it 2-1 against Rangers

It nearly got even better for the hosts five minutes before half-time when Moore's cross was met well by the towering Emmanuel Fernandez but his header flashed just wide.

Martin O'Neill made changes at half-time, introducing Sebastian Tounekti and Hatate in place of Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain and Junior Adamu but it was Rangers who started the half on the front foot.

Skov Olsen saw an effort deflected wide before Tuur Rommens could have done better after connecting with a Moore cross at the back post but he sent his effort straight at Sinisalo.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Kieran Tierney's solid header gets Celtic a goal back early in the second half of the Old Firm

Celtic did start to gain a foothold in the second half shortly after that, however, getting more of the ball and frustrating the hosts. Benjamin Nygren had a chance but fired well over the bar from the edge of the box before Hatate forced Butland into a good save from distance.

They were rewarded for their pressure just before the hour mark when Rangers failed to clear their lines, allowing Nygren to deliver a cross into the box where Tierney was unmarked and he powered a header back across Butland and into the net.

Image: Celtic's Reo Hatate celebrates scoring to make it 2-2 against Rangers

Celtic had a great chance to get back on level terms soon after when Maeda found some space in the box but he could only direct his header straight at Butland before Hatate fired over from the edge of the box.

The visitors really should have got their equaliser after 78 minutes following some brilliant build-up play, when Luke McCowan found himself in acres of space but his curling effort was smothered by Butland.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Dujon Sterling's handball gifts Celtic late penalty that Reo Hatate puts away to level the score in the Old Firm

Ibrox was nervy as the clock ticked down and Rangers fans thought they had witnessed another unbelievable Butland save from a Maeda header only for referee John Beaton to be called to the monitor with Sterling adjudged to have used his hand to deflect the Celtic forward's chance.

The Rangers goalkeeper produced a brilliant stop to deny Hatate from the spot and then again on the follow up but he could not prevent the midfielder eventually bundling the ball into the net to help Celtic secure a last gasp point in a result that will leave Hearts and Motherwell fans thrilled.

O'Neill: Celtic could have won

Celtic manager Martin O'Neill on Sky Sports:

"First half we were not good, we were second best. We were not even second best, we were about fourth best. Rangers got off to a great start, they were dominating proceedings, they got the second goal, not a great one from our viewpoint.

"At half-time you were thinking we had to show more determination, more spirit, more aggression and win some second balls and then having the ability to pass. Other than the first three or four minutes of the second half I thought we totally dominated the second period and we deserved something out of the game.

"And with the chances we carved out that we didn't take, we could easily have won.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Martin O'Neill reflects on Celtic's 2-2 draw in the Old Firm and believes his side are still very much in the title race

"The second half belonged to us and we were terrific. The crowd started to get concerned about their own team's performance, we are in the ascendancy and we played really well in the second half. You need determination to go and do it but we played some really good stuff in the second half, wave after wave. And this is at Ibrox as well so that gives us more heart.

"By contrast to quite a number of years ago, I am relatively calm. If it had been the old team I would have gone absolutely ballistic [at half-time] but this team, like the old team, find a way and they came roaring back.

"They showed a bit of determination and heart and that has been the case. It is the sign of champions. Generally speaking they have been in cruise control for the last couple of months of each season and this has been really difficult. We've been chasing the whole way through and that will remain the case."

Rohl: Rangers needed third goal

Rangers head coach Danny Rohl on Sky Sports:

"When you lead 2-0 you are very disappointed when you get a draw at the end. I think we played an outstanding first half, even the start of the second half, the first five or six minutes was really well run.

"We said 2-0 is always difficult because the opponent need one moment and then they can come back. In the second half we were not clinical in possession, too many chances for them to get the ball back.

"We said at half-time we wanted to continue the last line and we had some moments where we didn't, we lost the ball. They had some momentum with the 2-1 and they had nothing to lose. We are really disappointed today but next week we have to go again.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Rangers head coach Danny Rohl reflects on his sides' 2-2 draw in the Old Firm despite leading by two goals at half-time

"For me it was clear you need the 3-0. We had moments to do this and then finally they have nothing to lose and score the 2-1. Until the end you fight for this point and then it was lucky for them to get the penalty so late."

Rangers head coach Danny Rohl on Sky Sports on VAR and penalty incident:

"Maybe, maybe not, I think it is not really my part.

"The important thing is that every week you have good decision-making and today and last week were against us but this is football."

Football.title: Peter Fury to train Verhoeven ahead of Usyk fight
Football.content:
Peter Fury has confirmed he will be involved in Rico Verhoeven's training camp ahead of his shock fight with Oleksandr Usyk on May 23.

Heavyweight champion kickboxer Verhoeven is scheduled to take on three-time undisputed champion Usyk in a boxing match for the WBC heavyweight title in Giza, Egypt.

Fury, the uncle of Tyson Fury, will once again collaborate with Verhoeven to work on his boxing as he gears up for the second professional bout of his career.

Verhoeven has held the Glory heavyweight title for over 11 years as one of the greatest kickboxers in the history of the sport. His sole professional boxing match to date ended in a second-round knockout victory over Janos Finfera in April 2024.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Muay Thai fighter Liam Harrison believes no one can beat Usyk in boxing, as kickboxer Verhoeven prepares for their fight

"Quick update. Just arrived here in London," Fury said on X. "Going home and then back to work. Been away for three months in training camps and now we're off again to the biggest training camp, which is Oleksandr Usyk-Rico Verhoeven in Egypt at the Pyramids.

"It's going to be a great night, a lot of hard work to be done. Two great people, two great champions in their own right, what a privilege.

"Onwards we go, three months away in training camps, Spain, Holland now back to Holland for another three-month training camp."

Usyk will be returning to the ring for the first time since knocking out Daniel Dubois to win the undisputed heavyweight championship for a second time at Wembley Stadium last July.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Speaking on the Toe2Toe podcast, Fabio Wardley reveals why he thought Usyk vacated his WBO world title rather than face him as the mandatory challenger

Having been named undisputed champion at cruiserweight, Usyk repeated the feat at heavyweight in May 2024 when he defeated Tyson Fury by split decision, before following up with a unanimous-decision win in the pair's rematch later that December.

The WBC has allowed the undefeated Usyk a voluntary defence of its belt before he is required to face his mandatory challenger.

Agit Kabayel is Usyk's mandatory for the WBC belt after stopping Damian Knyba in January to extend his perfect record to 27-0 following wins over Zhilei Zhang, Frank Sanchez and Arslanbek Makhmudov, who is preparing to face Tyson Fury in April.
