News.title: Dramatic moment Putin’s shadow fleet tanker is seized by Belgium with help of France after ship was hit with sanctions
News.content:
THIS is the dramatic moment Belgian and French forces seize one of Putin’s sanctioned shadow fleet tankers in the North Sea.

The shocking video shows how helicopters surround the ship, which was carrying illegal Russian oil, using “a false flag and forged documents”.





Jaw-dropping footage shows how three helicopters and one speed boat rapidly surround the vessel.

A chopper hovers over the shadow fleet tanker, and one soldier can be seen abseiling from the aircraft to land on the deck below.

The trooper is quickly followed by two more, until a total of nine soldiers have boarded the ship.

Soldiers can then make their way across the tanker to take control of the rogue tanker.

Belgian Defence Minister Theo Francken confirmed that his military had taken control of the vessel called Ethera, which has been sanctioned for attempting to illegally export Russian oil.

He said in a post on X: “Over the past few hours, our armed forces, with the support of the French Defence, have boarded an oil tanker belonging to the Russian shadow fleet.”

“The vessel is currently being escorted to the port of Zeebrugge, where it will be seized.



The tankers help fund Mad Vlad’s assault on Ukraine by helping Russia to sell oil in defiance of international sanctions.

Francken added: “Without his shadow fleet Putin can’t wage war against innocent Ukrainians.

“So we take these vessels out. One by one. Till his war of aggression stops.”

Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky dubbed the fleet “Moscow’s floating purse” and called its capture “decisive”.

He wrote: “This particular vessel has long been under US, EU, and UK sanctions, but nonetheless continued to illegally transport Russian oil using a false flag and forged documents.



“We welcome this strong action against Moscow’s floating purse and thank France for supporting the operation.”

“It is also important to modernize European legislation so that tankers carrying Russian oil are not just stopped but seized, with their oil repurposed for Europe‘s security.



“We must be resolute. Russia operates like a mafia organization, and the response must match that reality.

“If they reject the rules for the sake of war, the rules must foresee a clear and firm answer.”

French president Emmanuel Macron called the boarding “a major blow to the shadow fleet”, adding: “Europeans are determined to cut off the sources of funding for Russia’s war of aggression in Ukraine by enforcing sanctions.”

News.title: Moment mindless drug driver admits taking ‘ket’ after ploughing into ‘kind’ motorcyclist, 22, while TEN times over limit
News.content:
THIS is the moment a ketamine-induced driver admits he was on the drug before mowing down and killing a motorcyclist.

Shocking footage shows 22-year-old motorist Cormac Sale slumped in the back of an ambulance after the fatal smash.





He had a blood pressure monitor strapped to his arm as paramedics ask if he had taken drugs or alcohol.

Shortly after he quietly replies, “ket,” confirming he had taken the drug that night.

He was later found to be almost 10 times over the legal limit on December 14, 2024 – the evening of the crash near Bolton.

Another chilling clip released by Greater Manchester Police shows the chilling moments before the fatal impact.



Dashcam footage shows Sale’s Skoda Fabia tearing along the road at speed with its headlights blazing.

The vehicle then appears to brake sharply as another driver pulls into a cycle lane.

Sale then accelerates and overtakes at speed, seconds before the catastrophic collision.

The motorcyclist Sale hit was a young lad called Spencer Rothwell-Poole.



Spencer, who was 22, died at the scene, leaving his devastated family shattered.

A moving statement was read by Spencers dad at Bolton Crown Court.

He said: “On the December 14, 2024, at 7pm I hugged my boy for the last time.

“I told him that I loved him and to stay safe, he went out on his bike and never came back, all because of one mindless person that night.

“Cormac Sale didn’t just kill my son.



“He killed the family.”

Spencer’s brother added: “He was an amazingly safe and careful motorbike rider, who did everything by the book.

“I miss him every day.”

Sale was jailed for 10 years and eight months after killing Spencer in the horrific crash near Bolton.

Detective Constable James Maskrey warned about the perilous risks of breaking the law and driving under the influence.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

He said: “This sentencing reflects the devastating consequences of choosing to drive recklessly and whilst under the influence.

“This case is a stark reminder that dangerous driving destroys lives.”

News.title: Three US troops killed and five injured in ‘Epic Fury’ Iran operation after Trump warned ‘heroes may be lost’
News.content:
THREE US troops have been killed in action during Donald Trump’s operation Epic Fury as Iran relentlessly blitzes Middle East nations.

Five have also been seriously wounded, the US military confirmed.





It said: “Several others sustained minor shrapnel injuries and concussions — and are in the process of being returned to duty.

Follow The Sun’s live blog for the latest updates on Iran

“Major combat operations continue and our response effort is ongoing.

“The situation is fluid, so out of respect for the families, we will withhold additional information, including the identities of our fallen warriors, until 24 hours after next of kin have been notified.”



The US military has not confirmed how or where the soldiers were killed.

It comes after Trump warned “American heroes may be lost” as the US and Israel yesterday unleashed mass strikes on Iran – sparking retaliation from Tehran.

He said: “The lives of courageous American heroes may be lost and we may have casualties, that often happens in war.

“We’re doing this, not for now, we’re doing this for the future, and it is a noble mission.



“We pray for every service member as they selflessly risk their lives to ensure that Americans and our children will never be threatened by a nuclear-armed Iran.”

The US president spent weeks building up a vast armada in the Middle East as he urged Tehran to cut a deal over its nuclear empire.

But early on Saturday Trump gave the green light for the US and Israel to launch an unprecedented blitz on the rogue nation.



Strikes have continued today after he vowed to “obliterate” the regime and “raze their missile industry to the ground”.

Trump warned Iran’s revolutionary guards not to retaliate yesterday as he said: “You must lay down your weapons and have complete immunity. Or in the alternative, face certain death.”

His threat fell on deaf ears, as within hours Tehran unleashed indiscriminate blitzes on US bases in the Middle East, and Gulf nations including Israel, Dubai and Bahrain.

It has pushed the Middle East to the brink of all-out war as Iran has insisted it will exact revenge after the killing of its supreme leader, Ali Khamenei.

It comes as…



Iran’s parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf said: “You have crossed our red line and must pay the price.

“We will deliver such devastating blows that you yourselves will be driven to beg.”

Today, a US warship came under attack from ballistic missiles fired from Iran – just hours after British troops downed an Iranian drone.

Iran’s revolutionary guards this afternoon said they had targeted Trump’s USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier with four ballistic missiles.

But the US military has confirmed the rockets failed to hit the carrier and “didn’t even come close”.



The USS Lincoln is one of two aircraft carriers deployed to the region in recent weeks, and the only one relatively close to Iranian shores.

Meanwhile, hundreds of British troops have been involved in near misses in Bahrain and Iraq, The Sun understands.

Earlier, troops in Bahrain were just 200 yards from an Iranian missile and drone strike on a US Naval base.

Footage on social media appeared to show US Patriot missiles failing to stop Iranian strikes on the port.



British troops in Iraq were just 400 metres from an airstrike that made it through allied defences.

All British troops are “safe and accounted for” defence insiders claimed – but they refused to say if any had been wounded.

While Iran struck US bases in Bahrain and the United Arab Emirates, the attacks have also hit outside of military installations, including a hotel in the Emirati city of Dubai, and Kuwaits international airport.

Oman, which has negotiated nuclear talks between Tehran and Washington, said it intercepted projectiles that appeared to target one of its seaports.

In repeated barrages across Israel, at least ten people were killed and more than 120 injured, according to authorities.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

Many missiles were intercepted, the military said.

Flights across the Middle East were disrupted, and air defense fire thudded over Dubai

News.title: How Iran will unleash last-ditch blitz with subs, mines & rockets as Trump’s attack ‘to last WEEKS’ to decapitate regime
News.content:
IRAN is lashing out with a bloody last-ditch blitz to save its regime after Donald Trump warned he will not stop his attack until it is obliterated.

Missiles, submarines, boats and sea mines have all been kicked into action as Tehran reels from the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.





Trump has vowed to keep bombing Iran until he has cut the head off the snake of the Islamic Republic’s regime after strikes yesterday wiped out at least 40 top commanders.

Follow The Sun’s live blog for the latest updates on Iran

Major General Jack Keane, Washington Institute for the Study of War director, has told The Sun that air strikes on Iran will continue for “weeks” until “the IRGC’s entire infrastructure is taken out”.

He said: “We have deliberate and methodical plans to strip away the IRGC.

“I’m not just talking about the leaders, I’m talking about the whole infrastructure that supports it, the technology that supports them.

“We’re talking about everybody in the system.”

The US president unleashed his wrath early on Saturday after repeated talks over the rogue nation’s nuclear empire failed to hit a breakthrough.



With the help of Israel, Trump ordered his military to bombard Iran in a mission – dubbed Epic Fury – to take down the regime.

Tehran has now been thrown into turmoil, as Khamenei’s surviving stooges desperately try to cling to diminishing power while grappling with external attacks and internal political uncertainty.

New terror chiefs are also vaulting into vacated positions in the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) command chain – and pressing on relentlessly with further waves of lethal attacks.

It has put the Middle East on the precipice of all-out war, threatening to spiral – and even draw in Russia, China and terrorist factions.

US Special Forces Colonel Ron McCammon, who fought in Afghanistan and other parts of Central Asia, told The Sun that the IRGC “is prepared for martyrdom” with a strategy based “entirely on terror”.



Despite Trump’s warning not to retaliate, Iran discharged countless indiscriminate missile and drone strikes against nearby Gulf countries and Israel within hours.

Refusing to back down although largely decimated by Trump’s continuing attacks, Iran’s remaining leaders are in the midst of a frenzied attempt to save its regime.

It comes as…

Missile launchers and storage bunkers in Iran survived the full day of intense US and Israeli air strikes – which have concentrated on decapitating Iran’s top leadership.

Iran’s defiant president Masoud Pexeshkian today insisted Tehran’s “bloodshed and revenge” is its “legitimate right and duty” – which he vowed to fulfil.



A massive volley of ballistic missiles streaked out of northwest Iran on midnight Saturday, hitting the centre of Israeli capital Tel Aviv and Dubai’s airport.

Merciless terror bosses are now deploying mini submarines, missiles, boats and sea mines to block the Straits of Hormuz.

It means as well as sparking death and destruction, Iran could also wreak havoc for the oil industry as some 20 per cent of the world’s oil passes through the strait.

Major international banks are warning that oil prices could double.



Although the US aircraft carrier and a dozen missile destroyers are stationed around the strategic waterway, the threat has scared surrounding states to suspend oil shipments.

Fresh shipping data today shows at least 150 tankers including crude and liquefied natural gas vessels dropped anchor in open Gulf waters beyond the strait.

Dozens more were stationary on the other side of the chokepoint.



It comes as more than 100 missiles and suicide drones have hit Qatar over the weekend and scores of others have exploded in the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Iraq.

Today, the US military confirmed three service members had been killed during operation Epic Fury.

Flames engulfed Abu Dhabi’s tallest high-rise after it was hit by Iran’s vicious Shahed kamikaze drones.

Other vulnerable infrastructure has also been struck and there is panic that desalination plants providing the main source of local drinking water are on the target list.

A radar installation is reported disabled at the Al Udeid air base in Qatar and the US 5th Fleet’s headquarters has been repeatedly struck in Bahrain.



But Iran’s bullying tactics on Gulf neighbours could backfire.

Fence-straddling Saudi Arabia declared that it would join forces with America and Israel against Iran after missiles struck its capital, Riyad.

But the IRGC calculates that if it can continue causing mayhem with its missile arsenal and massive quantities of Shahed 136 and more advanced 139 loitering drones, its terror proxies will join in with guerrilla.

“The Iranian military is very good at producing drones” general Keane said.

“That’s what they using against US bases. It’s a challenge.”



It could see Lebanon’s Hezbollah, Yemen’s Houthis and Iraq’s Al Sadr launch terror attacks against Israel, US bases and embassies and nervous Arab governments.

The IRGC’s ultra-secret unit 840, which controls terrorist sleeper cells around the world, operates from headquarters in Houthi-controlled Yemen.



British prime minister Keir Starmer said on Saturday that 20 terror plots uncovered in the UK over the past year are traced to Iranian-linked groups.

The FBI says it’s on “maximum alert” over terror threats in the US.

It comes as protests organised by a network of militant groups previously linked to violent pro-Hamas riots erupted in New York and other cities the moment air strikes were launched on Iran.

IRGC agents plotted with Mexican drug cartels to stage truck bombings in the US some years ago and recently tried to assassinate the Israeli ambassador in Mexico City.

Hezbollah cells are active throughout Latin America where terrorists involved in the massive 1992 bombing of a Jewish community in Argentina are currently based in Venezuela, according to US intelligence reports.



But most importantly, IRGC could also receive crucial backing from Russia and China – which have much to lose from a collapse of the regime.

Iran has been the main supplier of killer drones to Russia for its war on Ukraine and China receives 90 per cent of its oil.

Chinese military transports have recently been landing in Teheran, delivering parts and components to upgrade IRGC air defence systems with newly developed radar technology to detect stealth aircraft.

Just last week, Iran is understood to have reached a deal with China to acquire long-range CM-302 anti-ship cruise missiles designed to skim sea surfaces below radar detection.

While the US has disabled much of Iran’s Russian S-300 long range air defence batteries, Moscow is sneaking in hundreds of its newest Manportable Verba SAMs.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

These are equipped with three spectral infra-red and ultraviolet seekers capable of outsmarting countermeasures of US aircraft.

Russian has also been flying in Mi-28 “Havoc” helicopter gunships bristling with rockets and 30mm autocannons that could instantly slaughter crowds of anti-regime protestors.

News.title: ‘Inspiring’ restaurant manager, 26, suddenly dies as brother pays heartbreaking tribute
News.content:
AN “inspiring” restaurant manager has suddenly died, with her brother paying a heartbreaking tribute to her.

Charlotte Redsell took her own life at the young age of just 26.





Brother Stephen and Charlotte as well as their other siblings lost their mum when they were young and their father died during Covid.

Their traumatic childhood left them scarred with anxiety and health issues later in life.

Charlotte was found hanged at her flat in Strood, Kent, last October, having left a flurry of messages to friends and loved ones.

Her family are still mourning their loss and say Charlotte was “doing well”, was making plans to open her own business, preparing to take her driving test and looking forward to a Reiki class the following day.

Her death was felt by the local community who created an impromptu floral shrine outside Fish 55, where she worked.

Charlotte’s elder brother Stephen, 33, said it was now clear she had “suffered in silence”.

He told KentOnline: “I can see she was very protective of her emotions.”



Stephen, who works as a private chef, has battled a drinking problem but is proud that he has now been sober for two years.

Both Stephen and Charlotte turned to Mind, the mental health charity, for help.

He is now doing a Three Peaks challenge in September for the organisation along with his brother Peter, 32, and Charlotte’s pal Rachael.

Charlotte was only 10 when her mum died and none of the young family got any counselling.

Charlotte’s death though has made the siblings become even closer and they have been “reconnecting” with one another in their efforts to highlight the issues surrounding mental health.



Younger sister Chantelle, 24, has been particularly badly affected by Charlotte’s death as she was the one who found her at her home, after she became concerned about a message she had sent hours before.

After Charlotte left Chapter School, now Strood Academy, Stephen helped her out and used his contacts to get her a job in restaurants.

Stephen said she was a “natural” and quickly rose through the ranks to become front of house and a manager.

Then Stephen moved to Tunbridge Wells and she went with him and she got a job at Framptons in The Pantiles.

She also had a stint at Coast to Coast, an American diner in Medway Valley Park.



Stephen then got her a job at Fish at 55, a seafood restaurant in Rochester High Street, where he was a chef.

How to get help EVERY 90 minutes in the UK a life is lost to suicide It doesn’t discriminate, touching the lives of people in every corner of society – from the homeless and unemployed to builders and doctors, reality stars and footballers. It’s the biggest killer of people under the age of 35, more deadly than cancer and car crashes. And men are three times more likely to take their own life than women. Yet it’s rarely spoken of, a taboo that threatens to continue its deadly rampage unless we all stop and take notice, now. If you, or anyone you know, needs help dealing with mental health problems, the following organisations provide support: CALM, www.thecalmzone.net, 0800 585 858

Heads Together,www.headstogether.org.uk

HUMEN www.wearehumen.org

Mind, www.mind.org.uk, 0300 123 3393

Papyrus, www.papyrus-uk.org, 0800 068 41 41

Samaritans,www.samaritans.org, 116 123

He said: “I’m so proud of her. She loved her job, and it kept her busy.

“She was chasing her dreams of opening a coffee shop.”

Among the last words she told to people close to her were: “Some people don’t belong in this world – and that’s me”, an inquest at Oakwood House, Maidstone heard last week.

The court was also told that she had told her sister Chantelle that she loved her before saying what would be her final goodbye.



After her death on October 8, a toxicology report revealed she had alcohol and paracetamol in her blood.

There were no signs of injury or suspicious markings on her body to suggest third party involvement.

At a hearing on February 18, Ian Potter, the area coroner for Mid-Kent and Medway said: “I’m so sorry for your loss.

“At the age of 26, Charlotte was far too young.”

He recorded the cause of death as hanging and said he was satisfied from the messages on her phone that she had intended to take her own life.



Stephen has paid a touching tribute to his sister, describing her as “thoughtful” and “generous”.

He said: “She would give her last couple of quid to someone rather than see them go without.

“Charlotte may not be with is, but she can still be inspiring. Charlotte still has a voice.”

Charlotte was a popular figure, loved by the restaurant staff and customers.

On the morning of her death, the eaterie closed as a mark of respect.



Well-wishers, including neighbouring traders left floral tributes and candles at the restaurant, creating a touching shrine for her.

A notice read: “It is with the deepest regret that we share the passing of our friend and much-loved colleague, Charlotte Redsell, who sadly passed away this morning.

“Charlotte was a valued member of the Fish at 55 team and a cherished part of the wider Restaurants 4 Group family.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Our thoughts are with her family, friends, the team here, and all those who knew her – including our many customers and neighbouring businesses along Rochester High Street.”

Anyone wishing to contribute to Stephen’s Three Peaks challenge can do so here.

News.title: 'Someone parked in my space so I got the perfect revenge'
News.content:
'Someone parked in my space so I got the perfect revenge'

The woman owns a flat which has a private, residents-only car park attached to it but when she returned from being away, she found someone parked in the space she legally owns

View Image Brick pillar with private car park sign beside a gate and car in Manchester, UK.

A woman was enraged when someone parked in the private spot she pays for outside her flat, and it left her having to fork out for expensive on-street parking. The woman lives in a flat in a city centre, where parking is notoriously pricey.

‌



Where she lives, each flat comes with a parking space which is clearly numbered to show which flat the space belongs to. The parking space is included in the cost of the lease so a woman decided to take her revenge when she returned home and found someone parked in her spot.

‌



She took to Mumsnet with a post titled “Blocked in a car who parked in my space”, she explained: “To be clear I own my flat and the parking space is part of my lease and is legally mine.

‌



“If anyone wishes to use it they would need to have my permission as they would be trespassing otherwise. The space is on private land in a secure garage that requires fob access.”

She then said she came home from being away to “find a random car parked” in her space, and she has no clue who it belongs to. In a bid to avoid any potential drama, she initially paid to park “on the street outside for an hour hoping they would move soon but no one did”.

The woman continued: “Not wanting to spend any more money unnecessarily, I have now parked in front of my parking space which has blocked them in. It is not impacting anyone else’s space and allows free movement around the car park.

‌



“I have left a note on the car and put a message in the block’s group chat but no one has responded.”

She plans “to block them in” until she gets a response.

The woman was especially frustrated because city centre parking on the street costs £4 an hour and she has no way to determine when the offending car will move.

‌



At the time of writing her post, the car had been parked for at least three hours. The managing agent is closed over the weekend, so she couldn’t approach them for support in getting to the bottom of the issue.

She added: “As it’s a civil matter, I can’t call the police but according to Google, the car owner can call the police on me for blocking them in.

“I am also concerned in case they damage my car, again where would I stand legally if they did given that I have blocked them in?”

‌



In response, fellow Mumsnet users were keen to share their thoughts. One person simply said: “Stay put!”

Another said: “I don’t think the police are going to come to a private car park, with a phone number on the car so that the person can call to be unblocked. It’s likely to be a visitor who has misunderstood whose space is whose”.

They added: “I would assume someone has made a mistake, initially at least.”

‌



A third agreed: “Police will not care if a number has been left to contact you.”

But another erred on the side of caution and commented: “I really feel for you as it’s annoying and could be costly for you.

“But I think you are right about you being in the wrong for blocking them in. And there is a risk they’ll damage your car - possibly unlikely though.”

Article continues below

At the time of writing, 98% of Mumsnet users voted she wasn’t being unreasonable to block the offending car in.

News.title: 'Frustrated' Prince William 'wanted bombshell Andrew axe move instigated last year'
News.content:
'Frustrated' Prince William 'wanted bombshell Andrew axe move instigated last year'

It is said that Prince William had wanted to start the process of removing Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor from the line of succession at the same time the former Duke of York was stripped of his titles

View 5 Images Prince William with his uncle Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor (Image: Getty Images)

Prince William wanted Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor booted out of the line of succession last year, it has been reported.

‌



It is said that the Prince of Wales wanted the process instigated last autumn at the same time his disgraced uncle was stripped of his prince and Duke of York titles by the King. Mountbatten-Windsor, who was arrested last month on suspicion of misconduct in a public office, is eighth in line to the throne.

‌



He remains under police investigation after claims he shared confidential information during his time as UK trade envoy with paedophile financier Jeffrey Epstein. Following his arrest, the government has indicated it will consider legislation to remove the former prince from the line of succession.

‌



Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Andrew 'snubbed' by King Charles despite brother staying just two miles away READ MORE :

Princess Kate surprises with historic first as she and William issue new video READ MORE :

View 5 Images Andrew with the other royals before he was stripped of his titles (Image: Wiktor Szymanowicz/Future Publishing via Getty Images)

He can only be stripped of being in line to the throne by an Act of Parliament after agreement with the 14 British realms, which has the King as head of state. So far both Australia and New Zealand have thrown their weight behind the proposal, but government sources say the process could take years - and wouldn't be formally started until any police probes are over.

‌



And now the Sunday Times says William is frustrated by the delay in removing his uncle from the line of succession and is anxious about the implications of the scandal for his future reign. Just last week, while attending the BAFTAs with the Princess of Wales, three days after Mountbatten-Windsor's arrest, William remarked he was not in a calm state.

It came after he had admitted he had yet to watch the critically-acclaimed tearjerking movie Hamnet, as he needed "to be in quite a calm state" and added "I'm not at the moment". William is known not to be close to his shamed uncle, with the Mirror previously revealing that he demanded the ex-Duke of York be immediately banished from the royal fold “before the rot set in” following revelations of his close relationship with Epstein.

View 5 Images Andrew Mountbatten Windsor was arrested last month (Image: PA)

‌



According to a new biography of William and wife Kate by Mirror royal editor Russell Myers, the now Prince of Wales even appealed to his father and the late Queen to take drastic action in the aftermath of Andrew’s catastrophic BBC Newsnight interview in 2019, where he failed to apologise for associating with the convicted paedophile or recognise the effect on Epstein’s victims. Meanwhile, it has also been reported that no decision has yet been taken on whether Mountbatten-Windsor's daughters, Princess Beatrice or Princess Eugenie, will see a change to their status in the line of succession.

His eldest daughter Beatrice is ninth in line to the throne, followed by her two daughters, four-year-old Sienna Mapelli Mozzi and one-year-old Athena Mapelli Mozzi. Eugenie is in 12th place with her sons five-year-old August Brooksbank in 13th and one-year-old Ernest Brooksbank, 14th.

View 5 Images Andrew with his daughters Eugenie and Beatrice (Image: UK Press via Getty Images)

‌



Removing Andrew from the line of succession is said to be "constitutionally complex" and experts say legislation would have to explicitly state what would happen to his children and grandchildren's places in the line of succession. When the King stripped his younger brother of his titles last year, those of his nieces, Beatrice and Eugenie, were unaffected, with Charles said to be fond of the two women.

Today, the King was seen attending church on the Sandringham estate, less than a mile of where his brother has been holed up at Wood Farm since his arrest. He is currently living at the cottage as a temporary measure before, it is understood, that he will move to Marsh Farm, after being forced out of his previous vast mansion of Royal Lodge on the Windsor estate.

View 5 Images King Charles walks to church on the Sandringham estate today (Image: PA)

‌



But despite the brothers being in close proximity over the weekend, it is not believed that they met or have any plans to. Hours after Mountbatten-Windsor's arrest, the King put out a statement saying “the law must take its course” after expressing his “deepest concern”. He said: "I have learned with the deepest concern the news about Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and suspicion of misconduct in public office.

“What now follows is the full, fair and proper process by which this issue is investigated in the appropriate manner and by the appropriate authorities. In this, as I have said before, they have our full and wholehearted support and co-operation. Let me state clearly: the law must take its course.”

Article continues below

News.title: 'I'm a Brit trapped in Dubai with kids cowering from smoke and eerie bangs'
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: 'I'm a Brit trapped in Dubai with kids cowering from smoke and eerie bangs'

Dubai is the year-round-summer city where anyone can enjoy the lap of luxury. But in the space of 24 hours, it has been turned into a warzone with tourists and residents unable to flee

View 5 Images Fairmont The Palm hotel in Dubai was targeted (Image: INSTAGRAM)

Saturdays in Dubai usually are a time for sun, shopping and brunches - not bombs and missiles.

‌



But as the mercury hit 28C yesterday afternoon, the Emirates city - named 2025's 'second safest in the world' - was transformed into a zone of terror as drones hit the heart of its luxurious infrastructure. Influencers were left fleeing for their lives while tourists were crammed into underground carparks and residents were placed on lockdown.

‌



While bombing targets are usually places of military significance, Iran appears to have torn up the rule book and instead aimed for social hotspots that would have been packed with regular people. The 5-star Fairmont The Palm hotel on Dubai's man-made and eye-wateringly expensive Palm island was hit, injuring four people. Dubai airport and Jebel Ali Port were also pummelled while fire also broke out at the landmark Burj Al Arab after a drone was intercepted.

‌



Iran US LIVE: Three US service members dead and several injured in Operation Epic Fury READ MORE :

View 5 Images A suspected projectile falls over Dubai (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

The city, as a whole, is a playground for white collar workers who have emigrated en masse from the UK seeking better pay and sunnier climes. The likes of teachers, recruiters, PR workers, project managers and big pharma employees all benefit from tax-free earnings of up to £6,500 a month while living in the lap of luxury. From marble-clad malls packed with top designer stores to glitzy marinas and afternoons spent sipping cocktails on yachts, it's a champagne lifestyle that most can achieve.

‌



Added to that, the city is famously safe and their laws are infamously strict. Alcohol laws are strict in the mainly Muslim area. It can only be consumed by non-Muslims in licensed venues (hotels, restaurants, bars) and private homes. People must dress modestly in public spaces, covering their shoulders, and there is a zero-tolerance stance on drug possession. Last year it was named the second safest city in the world by Numero, topped only by nearby Abu Dhabi.

So the last thing residents and tourists would have been expecting yesterday was for missiles to start ringing overhead as plumes of acrid, black smoke billowed into the sky.

View 5 Images Dubai is known for its luxurious lifestyle (Image: Nikada)

‌



British influencer Will Bailey shared images from The Fairmont, and one tourist told how they were enjoying a cocktail in the sun on The Palm when the bomb hit. Not realising what it was, they carried on drinking. But it soon became all too clear how serious the danger was.

A British man teaching in Dubai, who wishes to remain anonymous, told how a regular Saturday suddenly transformed into scenes of panic and chaos. And as day turned into night, the missiles became chilling 'bangs' believed to be caused by interceptors breaking the sound barrier above their heads.

"It all went crazy yesterday with missiles flying over the community, and we were told to seek shelter," he tells The Mirror. "Then, as nighttime hit, so did a few large bangs. Apparently, they are missile interceptors breaking the sound barrier above our heads. We are about 5km from a military base that has anti-missile tech. No US military, though, so pretty safe."

‌



View 5 Images People look at smoke rising from the port of Jebel Ali in Dubai (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

As their new reality set in, mobile phones across the city rang out with terrifying warnings. Frightened for their lives, many spent the night lying awake while sleeping children were cocooned in the safest part of their houses.

"There were a few hairy moments in the night with warnings and sirens on our phones guiding us to seek shelter and move away from windows, and then followed with some noisy explosions. It woke me up; my partner hasn't slept all night, and we had our kids sleeping downstairs.

‌



"We thought it was all over this morning, but there have been lots more missiles and smoke above us. We hadn't heard anything for a few hours, then there were more happenings this afternoon."

View 5 Images British influencer Will Bailey showed the smoke from the Fairmont hotel in Dubai (Image: PICTURE TAKEN FROM TIKTOK)

With the city now on lockdown, he tells how there has been a Covid-style response with residents rushing to stock up on supplies. Flights across the Middle East have been grounded, and schools closed.

‌



"It's like Covid, we've stocked up on milk and toilet rolls. Airports are closed, and a few hotels have been hit by drones and debris. Schools are all remote learning until Thursday, so we're all indoors waiting it out."

Iran unleashed its weaponry after an early-morning attack by the US and Israel that killed its despotic leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Iran vowed swift vengeance and is also believed to have targeted areas with US military bases, including Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar and the UAE.

Dubai has no official US military base, but Jebel Ali Port is said to be the US Navy's entry to the Middle East and has American aircraft carriers and other naval vessels passing through.

News.title: Influencer Dubai terror from Love Island mum's panic to socialite hidden in bath
News.content:
Influencer Dubai terror from Love Island mum's panic to socialite hidden in bath

As events continue to develop in Dubai, the Mirror takes a look at the high-profile influencers now stranded in Dubai, who are now keeping followers updated on their harrowing experiences

View 12 Images A number of high-profile influencers have been left stranded in Dubai as tensions continue (Image: Instagram/vickypattison)

Usually a bustling hub frequented by glamorous travellers, Dubai Airport has now fallen silent, leaving a number of influencers stranded in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) city.

‌



A popular holiday destination for those fond of luxury living, it's no wonder that Dubai attracts so many social media stars, drawn to the city's striking skyline and array of opulent hotels. However, many will have woken up today in what may well feel like a very different place altogether.

‌



A shocking attack on Dubai International Airport, which saw smoke fill the halls of the usually packed hub, was followed by a fiery inferno at the luxurious Fairmont hotel. Blazes have also been reported at the famed Dubai landmark Burj Al Arab, and the Jebel Ali Port, while those in the area are urged to take shelter.

‌



Here, the Mirror takes a look at some of the well known influencers whose Dubai dream turned into a nightmare in just a few short hours.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

‌



Vicky Pattison

12 View 12 Images Vicky Pattison had been enjoying a holiday with her husband, Ercan Ramadan, when the chaos erupted (Image: Instagram/vickypattison) 12 View 12 Images The Strictly Come Dancing star told fans, 'We’re doing our best to stay calm' (Image: Instagram/vickypattison)

Former Strictly Come Dancing star Vicky Pattison had been enjoying a holiday with her husband, Ercan Ramadan, but the couple has since been left stranded just like so many other holidaymakers, as tensions continue to escalate. The I'm A Celeb winner had originally been set to travel from Dubai to Australia, and then on to New Zealand, before her flight was cancelled.

‌



"We are receiving a lot of messages from people asking if we’re ok which we are. We are currently in our hotel and have been assured we’re safe," she wrote on Instagram. "We are aware that the situation that is unfolding is scary and I’m also aware that there’s a lot of videos circulating on social media which are understandably concerning. I’m not sure what else to say, other than we’re doing our best to stay calm, not feed any unconfirmed narratives, and we're thinking of everyone who is feeling unsettled and unsafe right now."

Just one day before this sober update, Vicky and Ercan had enjoyed breakfast in "the world's highest outdoor infinity pool" at Zeta Seventy Seven at Dubai's Address Beach Resort. Appearing on cloud nine in a sweet Instagram vid, Vicky told followers: "Talk about bucket list type shiiiii... What an experience... and no, we didn't drop anything in the pool!!! I promise!" The day before, the couple had flown over to Dubai in style, in what Vicky described as their "first, first class experience". Showing excitement over their dreamy trip, Vicky revealed: "After how busy and chaotic the last year has been we really need this break together! And I'm so excited it's off to such a lush start!"

Laura Anderson

12 View 12 Images Love Island star Laura Anderson has opened up about the 'horrendous situation' (Image: Instagram/lauraanderson1x) 12 View 12 Images Mere hours before the explosions began, Laura shared a sweet clip and her and her daughter, Bonnie (Image: Instagram/lauraanderson1x)

‌



Love Island star Laura Anderson confirmed she and daughter Bonnie were safe in a social media post, telling followers, "Horrendous situation, Bonnie & I are fine", followed by a white heart emoji. She also reshared a heartfelt post from the page @mywildtribe about the additional fears that come with being a parent in these sorts of serious situations. The post reads: "Today, we heard the bombs - multiple loud explosions, the house shaking - and for the first time, the kids heard it too. And honestly, that's the moment it stops being news and starts feeling real. As mums, our first instinct is fear - not for ourselves, but for our children. You look at their faces and just want the world to stay normal for them, even when your heart is racing."

Laura, 36, also shared some helpful advice for others trapped in the UAE, including information on free of charge apartments for those stranded and, harrowingly, advice on the importance of taking shelter not just during a blast, but in the aftermath. What should have been a fun holiday took a change Laura didn't anticipate.

Mere hours before the worrying reports began, she had shared an adorable photo of herself and Bonnie at Glasgow Airport, just before takeoff. On their first morning in Dubai, Laura showed followers a clip of her hotel room, telling them: "Welcome to Dubai. Good morning! We woke up at 11.30, Bonnie is a lazy bones..." Turning to the swimming pool, Laura declared, "Can't wait to jump in there! Not, they didn't put the heating on, and it's freezing." After dipping an experimental toe in the water, however, she then turned to a pal, saying: "Oh, it's not cold!" before asking her young daughter whether she was happy to be in Dubai.

‌



Petra Ecclestone

View 12 Images Petra Ecclestone described last night as one of the 'worst' of her life (Image: @petraecclestoneofficial/Instagram)

F1 heiress Petra Ecclestone, who recently made the move to Dubai from LA with husband Sam Palmer and their children. Just one day before, she'd been celebrating daughter Lavinia's 13th birthday, out in the sunshine they'd longed for. Then everything changed.

‌



Opening up about the developing situation, the 37-year-old shared: "It was one of the most scary, worst nights actually of my life. I feel like us and the family have been through a lot recently for personal reasons. We came to Dubai to feel safe, and we finally felt like we were settling in, and now this has happened. So it's really kind of been a shocking turn of events.' But thank God we are safe. It was just really, yeah, not a great night. I hope everyone is staying safe, and we shall all get through this. I just want to say thank you so much.'

Petra also spoke about the difficulties of trying to explain the complex situation to her young children, Lavinia, James, Andrew and Minnie, admitting: "I don't have the answers to certain things, and that was terrible — just being asked what's going to happen next, whether it's going to stop, and hearing the bangs in the night. I think just trying to protect them and tell them that they're going to be okay. But then, as a mum, obviously, you don't have all the answers. So yeah, that was tough.

"I can't even imagine other people in history who have gone through wars and had to live through it day to day, like my dad, and this just being normal life. And then kids having to go to school with that sort of anxiety and worry. It was quite an eye-opening experience last night."

‌



Just last summer, in an interview with The Times, Petra spoke about how she felt so safe in Dubai. In a statement that now feels heartbreaking in light of this recent traumatic episode, Petra told the publication: "Dubai is one of my top places to travel with the children. I feel safer in Dubai than I do in LA or London. In London, my dad’s been robbed four times, my sister’s been burgled; it’s not the same place it used to be. LA is an absolute disaster, too, safety-wise. I sleep so much better in Dubai."

Petra and Sam had been living in a £31million LA mansion, while retaining their £170million Chelsea mansion, but regarded their move to Dubai as a "new era", often sharing photos of their adventures as a family.

Luisa Zissman

12 View 12 Images Luisa Zissman reached out to her 'fellow UAE gang' (Image: @luisazissman, /Instagram) 12 View 12 Images She urged people to 'stay safe' (Image: @luisazissman, /Instagram)

‌



Similar to Petra, Apprentice 2013 runner-up Luisa Zissman decided to up sticks to Dubai after tiring of the UK weather and concerns over crime. Leaving behind the multi-million pound Grade II-listed Hertfordshire home, which she and husband Andrew bought shortly after their 2015 wedding, Luisa took two of her three children, along with her horses, to join Andrew - who had already relocated - for a new life in the Middle East.

She announced the news on Instagram and responding to a comment that read, "everyone's leaving the UK now,", she agreed, "very sad have always loved the UK but looking forward to not being cold & being able to walk alone at night safely." Sadly for Luisa, her hopes of a safer life have been destroyed.

In an Instagram story update, Luisa wrote: 'Lots of messages re: Dubai. Lots of bangs we are hearing. Stay safe, fellow UAE gang." She also made sure to share information with her fellow British Nationals in the UAE, urging them to register their presence. Later on, Luisa revealed how she was keeping her little ones busy during this difficult time, with some old-fashioned home baking.

‌



Sharing photographs of their efforts taken in her kitchen, Luisa said: "Home-baked bread rolls. Keeping the kids entertained and indoors. We got itchy feet and went to take them to the park and literally as we went to step foot out the door we heard 2 massive explosions that shook the house, we retreated and then heard another 2. So now movie time in the basement. So surreal and scary. I do have faith that UAE defence will keep us all safe."

Sam Gowland

12 View 12 Images Geordie Shore star Sam Gowland shared some shocking footage from Dubai (Image: @samgowland/Instagram) 12 View 12 Images Describing the explosions, Sam said he'd 'never heard a noise like it before' (Image: @samgowland/Instagram)

‌



Just one week ago, former Geordie Shore star Sam Gowland shared snaps of his brand new Dubai house with his Instagram followers, revealing: "After 4 months stress & deep pockets I'm proud to announce my latest home/villa in Dubai is complete." Then, just two days ago, Sam, 30, announced he would be putting the multi-million renovation on the market, for a cool 10 million AED (£2 million).

Sadly, the ex Love Island contestant has now spoken out about the city for a very different reason. Sharing startling footage before a report broke of a missile attack on the Fairmont Hotel on the Palm Jumeirah, Sam shared footage of rockets soaring through the sky while he was on the beach, saying: "Wow... No we'll have to go. That's two or three now", including the caption, "Right above us on the Palm in Dubai today, bloody scary I tell you that, never heard a noise like it before."

In later footage, Sam appeared panicked as he exclaimed: "What the f*** is that? I've got it on video. F***ing hell," before adding: "Rocket above my house, WTF this is crazy." In the background, a worried female voice can be heard repeatedly saying, "I'm scared". In an update given this morning, Sam shared: "Wow, so scary last night, hardly slept with all the banging. This morning's wake-up call was at 7:30 am with two more rockets going over my villa, going to be another crazy day! Can't really tell you the feelign you get from the noisees I've never experienced anything like it [sic]."

‌



Hofit Golan

12 View 12 Images Hofit Golan saw 'rockets and explosions' from her hotel balcony (Image: Instagram/hofitgolanofficial) 12 View 12 Images The 40-year-old arrived in Dubai mere hours before the strikes began (Image: Instagram/hofitgolanofficial)

Israeli socialite Hofit Golan also shared footage from her hotel balcony capturing rockets and explosions striking the hotel, having arrived in Dubai mere hours earlier. She posted on Instagram: "After being debriefed from my flights as the skies closed above us. I came home to see the Palm filling up with smoke! I can see rockets and explosions from my balcony!"

‌



The 40-year-old went on to reveal that she'd taken shelter in her bathtub after witnessing the "surreal" moment, recalling: 'I heard loud explosions, and I thought 'I can't believe it, I am in the safest city in the world, and I can't believe this is happening'. Of course, I am frightened. It is a natural reaction. The UAE is supposed to be one of the safest places. You see it (explosions) in front of your balcony. You see fireworks usually, but that's a different kind of lighting of the skies."

She continued: "So, in the back of your head, it is like what is going to happen at night? Are we all going to be able to sleep? You kind of black out a little bit. I haven't caught my breath yet. I feel a bit shocked, a little overwhelmed and exhausted. They (The UAE) are successfully intercepting the missiles and doing their job well, but of course, it is a human natural emotion to be like 'oh gosh, oh my god', that is where I am at.

"I did not see anyone get hurt or hear shouting. I can't think straight. I am just processing the emotions. It's kind of surreal. We have been getting reports that they are shutting down the tourist places and we're being told to stay indoors and stay away from windows."

Article continues below

Like this story? For more of the latest showbiz news and gossip, follow Mirror Celebs on TikTok , Snapchat , Instagram , Twitter , Facebook , YouTube and Threads . Do you have a story to share? Email me at julia.banim@reachplc.com

News.title: Brits in Middle East urged to register presence with foreign office amid Iran strikes
News.content:
The UK government is urging British nationals across the Middle East to register their presence with the foreign office as Iran continues its assault on neighbouring countries.

Since Saturday (February 28), violence has erupted across the Middle East, ignited by what the US is calling 'Operation Epic Fury' with its joint military strikes with Israel against Iran.

In what appears to be revenge attacks, Iran has sent its own missiles raining down across the region, hitting Dubai, Doha, Bahrain and Kuwait in a bid to target US air bases.

As the situation continues to evolve, the Foreign Commonwealth & Development Office has issued fresh guidance and is advising "against all but essential travel" to Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar and the UAE.

US-Israeli missiles struck and killed supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in the carnage yesterday. Iran has since hit back, aiming for US bases but also apparently striking other landmarks - such as luxury hotel The Fairmont in Dubai's Palm Jumeriah.

The FCDO says Brits in Bahrain, Israel, Kuwait, Palestine, Qatar and UAE "should shelter in place, avoid travel where possible and follow instructions from the local authorities".

In addition, it is urging UK nationals to "register your presence" with the foreign office to "receive direct updates" about the situation in the Middle East.

"British nationals across the Middle East should monitor our travel advice, read our guidance on being affected by a crisis abroad, and follow advice from the local authorities."

In the past hour, it added any visiting Salalah, or within 100km, "should look to leave as soon as possible if commercial means allow".

News.title: UK's 10 'most dangerous' areas named with two areas in one city topping the list
News.content:
UK's 10 'most dangerous' areas named with two areas in one city topping the list

Latest crime statistics from the City Safety Index reveal the 10 UK areas with the highest crime rates, with two areas in one city claiming the top spots

View 3 Images Some areas in Britain are highly dangerous (Image: Getty)

According to the latest data from Get Licensed, the UK's premier security training provider, 10 locations warrant extra vigilance. The City Safety Index has ranked Manchester as the tenth most dangerous city in the UK.

‌



Despite an average crime rate of 152 per 1,000 residents, this northern powerhouse has seen a marked improvement compared to last year.

‌



Leicester, meanwhile, holds the dubious honour of being the ninth most dangerous city in Britain, with Newcastle upon Tyne in eighth place and Middlesbrough taking seventh place. Blackpool, however, outstrips them all, securing sixth place on the list of riskiest locations.

‌



Bristol, steeped in history, has clocked up a crime rate just below that of London's Westminster borough, making it only slightly safer.

Nottingham ranks among the top three most hazardous locations in Britain. With an annual crime rate of roughly 118 per 1,000 residents across postcodes NG1 to NG34, it ranks among the top three most dangerous areas, according to the Express.

View 3 Images There are 10 locations where heightened awareness is advisable (Image: boonchai wedmakawand via Getty Images)

‌



Despite the allure of certain parts of the Capital, Islington has been singled out as the "second least safe place in Great Britain".

This is particularly true for postcodes N1, N4 to N8, N16 and N19, where robberies and thefts are the most commonly reported crimes.

In fact, theft and robbery figures for the area are running at "three times higher than the national average". Crime in the neighbourhood has surged by more than 8% compared to the previous year, while reoffending rates continue to sit at worrying levels.

‌



Topping the list as Britain's "most dangerous" area is the London borough of Camden.

View 3 Images Despite its desirability, Islington is high on crime (Image: Getty)

Despite Camden's enduring popularity with both locals and tourists alike, the area has gained notoriety for incidents of harassment, antisocial behaviour, and violent disorder.

Article continues below

Postcodes spanning the Camden borough - especially N1, N6, N7, N19, and NW1 through to NW8 - have been flagged as the highest-risk zones. What's more, offenders operating within Camden show a markedly high likelihood of reoffending.

Top 10 most dangerous areas in Britain:

News.title: Nigel Farage accused of 'trying to rig' election rules in Trumpian 'sore loser' move
News.content:
Nigel Farage accused of 'trying to rig' election rules in Trumpian 'sore loser' move

Nigel Farage is accused of trying to 'rig the rules' in elections with Trumpian plans to restrict who can vote and ban postal votes after defeat at the Gorton and Denton by-election

View 2 Images Nigel Farage was accused of invoking Donald Trump's playbook (Image: Christopher Furlong, Getty Images)

Nigel Farage has been accused of trying to “rig the rules” in elections after unveiling Trumpian plans to restrict who can vote and ban postal votes.

‌



TUC chief Paul Nowak branded the Reform UK leader a “sore loser” who was peddling “baseless conspiracies” after he was defeated by the Green Party in the Gorton and Denton by-election last week.

‌



Writing in the Mail on Sunday, Mr Farage claimed the Greens’ victory “was the most glaring example yet of what happens if we’re not careful about the impact of mass immigration and the legitimacy of those who can vote in our elections”.

‌



Reform announced on Sunday that it would only allow naturalised British citizens to vote in British Parliamentary elections. Currently, Commonwealth citizens of British Crown dependencies and British overseas territories who have leave to enter or remain in the UK can vote.

View 2 Images Reform candidate Matt Goodwin, with Nigel Farage above, beat Keir Starmer’s party at the Gorton and Denton by-election (Image: PA)

Mr Farage also vowed to end postal voting, with exemptions for the elderly, disabled people, serving armed forces personnel and those working overseas during an election.

‌



Mr Nowak told The Mirror: “Nigel Farage is a sore loser. His baseless conspiracy theories make him sound more like Donald Trump every day. Instead of trying to rig the rules - restricting who can vote or banning postal ballots - he should look closer to home.

“People in Gorton and Denton turned out to reject Reform’s nasty and divisive agenda. An agenda that would scrap employment protections, legalise discrimination and axe renters' rights - handing more power to bad bosses and rogue landlords.”

Reform beat Keir Starmer ’s party in the Labour safe seat in Greater Manchester but Green candidate Hannah Spencer stormed to victory with a more than 4,000 majority.

‌



Since the result, Mr Farage has invoked Donald Trump’s playbook and claimed his defeat was a result of “election fraud”, amid concerns of family voting. Family voting is an illegal practice where two voters use one polling booth and potentially direct each other or collude on voting.

On Friday, Reform reported cases of family voting to the electoral commission and the police. Mr Farage said the allegations raise “serious questions about the integrity of the democratic process in predominantly Muslim areas”, adding: "If this is what was happening at polling stations, just imagine the potential for coercion with postal votes.”

Democracy Volunteers said its observers saw family voting in 15 of the 22 polling stations observed in the Gorton and Denton by-election.

Article continues below

Manchester City Council said no issues had been reported and that it was "extremely disappointing" the group had waited until after the close of polls to make the claims.

Last week, a Greens spokesperson said: "This is an attempt to undermine the democratic result and is straight out of the Trump playbook.

"We've just won a historic by-election by a comfortable margin. "We've shown the country that Greens can beat Reform, despite their big business donations."

News.title: Keir Starmer's 'deep concern' over Iran attacks on civilian sites as No10 issues update
News.content:
Keir Starmer's 'deep concern' over Iran attacks on civilian sites as No10 issues update

Keir Starmer has expressed 'solidarity' with the leaders of several Middle Eastern countries 'in the face of dangerous Iranian escalation' following joint US-Israeli air strikes

View 3 Images The Prime Minister also expressed 'deep concern' about attacks on civilian sites in a call with leaders in the Middle East (Image: ANDY RAIN/POOL/EPA/Shutterstock)

Keir Starmer has expressed "solidarity" with the leaders of several Middle Eastern countries "in the face of dangerous Iranian escalation" following joint US-Israeli air strikes.

‌



In an update on Sunday afternoon, Downing Street said the Prime Minister had spoken to the kings of Jordan and Bahrain as well as the crown prince of Kuwait this morning as strikes continued across the region.

‌



Explosions have been heard in multiple Gulf states as Iran vowed to carry out its "most intense operation" ever in retaliation for the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

‌



Speaking to the three leaders, Mr Starmer expressed "deep concern" about attacks on civilian sites, and discussed ensuring the safety of British nationals and military personnel. And he told the nations the UK has "planes in the sky" to defend its partners in the region.

Iran US LIVE: Three US service members dead and several injured in Operation Epic Fury READ MORE :

View 3 Images A plume of smoke rises following a reported Iranian strike in Dubai (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Referring to the call, a Downing Street spokesman said: "They discussed the safety of civilians in the region, including British nationals and armed forces personnel, and expressed deep concern that strikes have hit civilian and commercial infrastructure - harming innocent people.

‌



“He reiterated that the UK has had planes in the sky since yesterday as part of the coordinated defensive response to protect our partners in the region, and would continue to offer our support.”

Yesterday, Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper condemned "indiscriminate Iranian strikes" after a fire broke out at a luxury hotel in Dubai. In a statement on X, Ms Cooper said she had spoken to her Emirati counterpart Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan "to express our solidarity with the UAE". She said: "The scenes in the Palm Hotel in Dubai are awful. The UK condemns these indiscriminate Iranian strikes."

The UK has played no part in the US-Israeli strikes, but Defence Secretary John Healey warned on Sunday that Iranian retaliation could put UK personnel and allies at risk. On Sunday, he said Iranian strikes landed within "a few hundred yards" of British troops in Bahrain. And he also confirmed UK forces have shot down Iranian drones in northern Iraq in the last 24 hours.

‌



View 3 Images Defence Secretary John Healey said Iranian strikes landed within "a few hundred yards" of British troops in Bahrain (Image: Sky News)

Mr Healey also confirmed two ballistic missiles capable of causing significant damage were fired towards Cyprus on Saturday. He said the Ministry of Defence does not believe the missiles were "targeted” at the island. But he said Iran was becoming "increasingly indiscriminate, widespread and uncontrolled" in its attacks.

Mr Healey said: "When I talk about increasingly indiscriminate attacks, I know people at home will be worried that not just military targets were hit last yesterday, but also we've seen hotels in Dubai and Bahrain. We've seen Kuwait's civilian airport. But in that Bahrain military base that was hit by missiles and drones yesterday, we had 300 British personnel, some within several hundred yards of the strike."

‌



He also warned that Iranian missile commanders were "increasingly allowed to choose their own targets" as the country was "losing its command and control".

The Defence chief said defensive British aircraft in the Middle East will "take down" Iranian missiles and drones "menacing" UK bases and allies. He added: "I can confirm that we've got a counter drone team in northern Iraq and they took down Iranian drones during the course of the last 24 hours."

On the death of Khamenei, Mr Healey said: "I think few people will mourn the Ayatollah's death, not least the family and friends of those thousands of young protesters murdered on the streets of Iranian cities. Iran and the regime he's led for so long, it's a source of evil, murdering its own citizens and sponsoring and exporting terror, including to countries like Britain."

‌



But Mr Healey refused to say whether the UK thought the US strikes were legal. He said: "Britain played no part in the strikes on Iran. We share, however, the primary aim of all allies in the region and the US that Iran should never have a nuclear weapon." He later added: "It is for the US to set out the legal basis of the action that it took."

Mr Healey also declined to say whether the UK had refused the US permission to use British bases to strike Iran but said: "What I can say is we've stepped up alongside the Americans. We've stepped up our defensive forces in the Middle East.”

The Government, along with France and Germany, has condemned Iran's retaliation and urged Tehran to return to the negotiating table. But other allies have gone further, with both Canada and Australia saying they supported the US "acting to prevent Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon" and continuing to threaten "international peace and security".

Article continues below

Green Party leader Zack Polanski criticised the Government for refusing to say whether it supported Donald Trump ’s strikes or not. He said: "We've seen what I would say is an illegal and unprovoked attack. We've got a Defence Secretary saying that diplomacy is the long runway we need but won't condemn Donald Trump when he attacks a country and assassinates its leader. That's the law of the jungle. That's an end to international law."

But Tory shadow foreign secretary Priti Patel hit out at ministers for not being more "proactive" in backing the strikes.

News.title: The months of planning behind US-Israeli mission to target Iran's supreme leader
News.content: Iran knew the supreme leader was in the sights of its enemies and so the failure to identify and deal with these vulnerabilities in the intervening months suggests a deep failure for Iranian security and counterintelligence or else the ability of Israel and the US to continue to adapt their methods to find new ways of tracking.

News.content: The Gulf states had made efforts to ease tensions with Iran in recent years,. They have worked hard to mediate a diplomatic solution to the crisis and have refused to let the US launch attacks from its bases in their countries.
